import json
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Annotated

import yaml
from rich.console import Console
from typer import Argument, Option, Typer

from harbor.cli.utils import (
    load_mcp_servers,
    parse_env_vars,
    parse_kwargs,
    parse_tpu_spec,
    resolve_environment_spec,
    run_async,
    warn_deprecated_flag,
)
from harbor.models.agent.name import AgentName
from harbor.models.environment_type import EnvironmentType
from harbor.models.trial.config import (
    EnvironmentConfig,
    ResourceMode,
    TaskConfig,
    TrialConfig,
)

trials_app = Typer(
    no_args_is_help=True, context_settings={"help_option_names": ["-h", "--help"]}
)
console = Console()

_AGENT_METAVAR = (
    "[" + "|".join(sorted(AgentName.values() - {AgentName.ACP.value})) + "|acp:<agent>]"
)
_ENV_METAVAR = "[" + "|".join(sorted(e.value for e in EnvironmentType)) + "]"


@trials_app.command()
def start(
    path: Annotated[
        Path | None,
        Option(
            "-p",
            "--path",
            help="Path to a local task directory, or path within git repo if --task-git-url "
            "is specified",
            rich_help_panel="Task",
        ),
    ] = None,
    config_path: Annotated[
        Path | None,
        Option(
            "-c",
            "--config",
            help="A trial configuration path in yaml or json format. "
            "Should implement the schema of sandbox.models.trial.config:TrialConfig. "
            "Allows for more granular control over the trial configuration.",
            rich_help_panel="Config",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    trial_name: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--trial-name",
            help="Name of the trial (default: auto-generated)",
            rich_help_panel="Trial Settings",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    trials_dir: Annotated[
        Path | None,
        Option(
            "--trials-dir",
            help="Directory to store trial results (default: ./trials)",
            rich_help_panel="Trial Settings",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    timeout_multiplier: Annotated[
        float | None,
        Option(
            "--timeout-multiplier",
            help="Multiplier for task timeouts (default: 1.0)",
            rich_help_panel="Trial Settings",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    agent_timeout_multiplier: Annotated[
        float | None,
        Option(
            "--agent-timeout-multiplier",
            help="Multiplier for agent execution timeout (overrides --timeout-multiplier)",
            rich_help_panel="Trial Settings",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    verifier_timeout_multiplier: Annotated[
        float | None,
        Option(
            "--verifier-timeout-multiplier",
            help="Multiplier for verifier timeout (overrides --timeout-multiplier)",
            rich_help_panel="Trial Settings",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    agent_setup_timeout_multiplier: Annotated[
        float | None,
        Option(
            "--agent-setup-timeout-multiplier",
            help="Multiplier for agent setup timeout (overrides --timeout-multiplier)",
            rich_help_panel="Trial Settings",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    environment_build_timeout_multiplier: Annotated[
        float | None,
        Option(
            "--environment-build-timeout-multiplier",
            help="Multiplier for environment build timeout (overrides --timeout-multiplier)",
            rich_help_panel="Trial Settings",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    agent_name: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "-a",
            "--agent",
            metavar=_AGENT_METAVAR,
            help=(
                "Agent to run, or a custom agent import path "
                "(module.path:ClassName). Also accepts an ACP registry "
                "shorthand (e.g. acp:opencode@1.3.9)."
            ),
            rich_help_panel="Agent",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    agent_import_path: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--agent-import-path",
            help="Deprecated: pass the import path to --agent instead.",
            rich_help_panel="Agent",
            show_default=False,
            hidden=True,
        ),
    ] = None,
    model_name: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "-m",
            "--model",
            help="Model name for the agent",
            rich_help_panel="Agent",
            show_default=True,
        ),
    ] = None,
    agent_timeout_sec: Annotated[
        float | None,
        Option(
            "--agent-timeout",
            help="Agent execution timeout in seconds (overrides task default)",
            rich_help_panel="Agent",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    agent_setup_timeout_sec: Annotated[
        float | None,
        Option(
            "--agent-setup-timeout",
            help="Agent setup timeout in seconds (overrides default)",
            rich_help_panel="Agent",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    agent_kwargs: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--agent-kwarg",
            help="Additional agent kwarg in the format 'key=value'. You can view "
            "available kwargs by looking at the agent's `__init__` method. "
            "Can be set multiple times to set multiple kwargs.",
            rich_help_panel="Agent",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    allow_agent_hosts: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--allow-agent-host",
            help="Run-specific hostname merged into the agent phase allowlist "
            "during agent.run() only. Can be used multiple times.",
            rich_help_panel="Agent",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    agent_env: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--ae",
            "--agent-env",
            help="Environment variable to pass to the agent in KEY=VALUE format. "
            "Can be used multiple times. Example: --ae AWS_REGION=us-east-1",
            rich_help_panel="Agent",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    agent_include_logs: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--agent-include-logs",
            help="Glob pattern of files to download from the agent logs "
            "directory, relative to it. Can be used multiple times.",
            rich_help_panel="Agent",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    agent_exclude_logs: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--agent-exclude-logs",
            help="Glob pattern of files to skip when downloading the agent "
            "logs directory, relative to it. Applied after includes. "
            "Can be used multiple times.",
            rich_help_panel="Agent",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    mcp_config: Annotated[
        list[Path] | None,
        Option(
            "--mcp-config",
            help="Path to a Claude-style .mcp.json or Harbor MCP config file. Can be used multiple times.",
            rich_help_panel="Agent",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    skills: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--skill",
            "--skills",
            help="Path or git source (org/name[@ref], URL) for skill directories. "
            "Can be used multiple times.",
            rich_help_panel="Agent",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    environment: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "-e",
            "--env",
            metavar=_ENV_METAVAR,
            help=(
                f"Environment type (default: {EnvironmentType.DOCKER.value}) or a "
                "custom environment import path (module.path:ClassName)."
            ),
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    environment_type: Annotated[
        EnvironmentType | None,
        Option(
            "--environment-type",
            help="Deprecated: pass the environment type to --env instead.",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
            hidden=True,
        ),
    ] = None,
    allow_environment_hosts: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--allow-environment-host",
            help="Run-specific hostname merged into the [environment] network "
            "baseline at agent env start. Can be used multiple times.",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    environment_import_path: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--environment-import-path",
            help="Deprecated: pass the import path to --env instead.",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
            hidden=True,
        ),
    ] = None,
    environment_force_build: Annotated[
        bool | None,
        Option(
            "--force-build/--no-force-build",
            help=f"Whether to force rebuild the environment (default: {
                '--force-build'
                if EnvironmentConfig.model_fields['force_build'].default
                else '--no-force-build'
            })",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    environment_delete: Annotated[
        bool | None,
        Option(
            "--delete/--no-delete",
            help=f"Whether to delete the environment after completion (default: {
                '--delete'
                if EnvironmentConfig.model_fields['delete'].default
                else '--no-delete'
            })",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    cpus: Annotated[
        ResourceMode | None,
        Option(
            "--cpus",
            help="How to apply task CPU resources: auto, limit, request, guarantee, or ignore.",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    memory: Annotated[
        ResourceMode | None,
        Option(
            "--memory",
            help="How to apply task memory resources: auto, limit, request, guarantee, or ignore.",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    override_cpus: Annotated[
        int | None,
        Option(
            "--override-cpus",
            help="Override the number of CPUs for the environment",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    override_memory_mb: Annotated[
        int | None,
        Option(
            "--override-memory-mb",
            help="Override the memory (in MB) for the environment",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    override_storage_mb: Annotated[
        int | None,
        Option(
            "--override-storage-mb",
            help="Override the storage (in MB) for the environment",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    override_gpus: Annotated[
        int | None,
        Option(
            "--override-gpus",
            help="Override the number of GPUs for the environment",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    override_tpu: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--override-tpu",
            help=(
                "Override the TPU spec for the environment in TYPE=TOPOLOGY "
                "format (e.g. 'v6e=2x4'). The task allocates one TPU slice "
                "per pod, so only a single spec is accepted."
            ),
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    mounts: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--mounts",
            "--mounts-json",
            help="JSON array of volume mounts for the environment container "
            "(Docker Compose service volume format). --mounts-json is a "
            "deprecated alias.",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    extra_docker_compose: Annotated[
        list[Path] | None,
        Option(
            "--extra-docker-compose",
            help="Additional Docker Compose overlay file. Can be used multiple times.",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    environment_kwargs: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--environment-kwarg",
            help="Environment kwarg in key=value format (can be used multiple times)",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    verifier_timeout_sec: Annotated[
        float | None,
        Option(
            "--verifier-timeout",
            help="Verifier execution timeout in seconds (overrides task default)",
            rich_help_panel="Verifier",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    verifier_env: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--ve",
            "--verifier-env",
            help="Environment variable to pass to the verifier in KEY=VALUE format. "
            "Can be used multiple times. Example: --ve OPENAI_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8000/v1",
            rich_help_panel="Verifier",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    verifier_include_logs: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--verifier-include-logs",
            help="Glob pattern of files to download from the verifier logs "
            "directory, relative to it. Can be used multiple times.",
            rich_help_panel="Verifier",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    verifier_exclude_logs: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--verifier-exclude-logs",
            help="Glob pattern of files to skip when downloading the verifier "
            "logs directory, relative to it. Applied after includes. "
            "Can be used multiple times.",
            rich_help_panel="Verifier",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    verifier: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--verifier",
            help="Custom verifier import path (module.path:ClassName).",
            rich_help_panel="Verifier",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    verifier_import_path: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--verifier-import-path",
            help="Deprecated: pass the import path to --verifier instead.",
            rich_help_panel="Verifier",
            show_default=False,
            hidden=True,
        ),
    ] = None,
    verifier_kwargs: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--verifier-kwarg",
            help="Additional verifier kwarg in the format 'key=value'.",
            rich_help_panel="Verifier",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    task_git_url: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--task-git-url",
            help="Git URL for a task repository",
            rich_help_panel="Task",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    task_git_commit_id: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--task-git-commit",
            help="Git commit ID for the task (requires --task-git-url)",
            rich_help_panel="Task",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
):
    """Start a single trial."""
    from harbor.models.trial.result import TrialResult
    from harbor.trial.trial import Trial

    base_config = None
    if config_path is not None:
        if config_path.suffix == ".yaml":
            base_config = TrialConfig.model_validate(
                yaml.safe_load(config_path.read_text())
            )
        elif config_path.suffix == ".json":
            base_config = TrialConfig.model_validate_json(config_path.read_text())
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported config file format: {config_path.suffix}")

    if base_config is None:
        if path is None:
            raise ValueError("Either --path or --config must be provided")

        config = TrialConfig(
            task=TaskConfig(path=path),
            trial_name=trial_name or "",
            trials_dir=trials_dir or Path("./trials"),
            timeout_multiplier=timeout_multiplier or 1.0,
        )
    else:
        config = base_config

    if trials_dir is not None:
        config.trials_dir = trials_dir
    if timeout_multiplier is not None:
        config.timeout_multiplier = timeout_multiplier
    if agent_timeout_multiplier is not None:
        config.agent_timeout_multiplier = agent_timeout_multiplier
    if verifier_timeout_multiplier is not None:
        config.verifier_timeout_multiplier = verifier_timeout_multiplier
    if agent_setup_timeout_multiplier is not None:
        config.agent_setup_timeout_multiplier = agent_setup_timeout_multiplier
    if environment_build_timeout_multiplier is not None:
        config.environment_build_timeout_multiplier = (
            environment_build_timeout_multiplier
        )

    if agent_import_path is not None:
        warn_deprecated_flag("--agent-import-path", "--agent")
        config.agent.import_path = agent_import_path
        config.agent.name = None
    if agent_name is not None:
        config.agent.name = agent_name
        config.agent.import_path = None  # --agent wins over deprecated alias
    if model_name is not None:
        config.agent.model_name = model_name
    if agent_timeout_sec is not None:
        config.agent.override_timeout_sec = agent_timeout_sec
    if agent_setup_timeout_sec is not None:
        config.agent.override_setup_timeout_sec = agent_setup_timeout_sec
    if agent_kwargs is not None:
        config.agent.kwargs.update(parse_kwargs(agent_kwargs))
    if allow_agent_hosts is not None:
        config.agent.extra_allowed_hosts.extend(allow_agent_hosts)
    if agent_env is not None:
        config.agent.env.update(parse_env_vars(agent_env))
    if agent_include_logs is not None:
        config.agent.include_logs.extend(agent_include_logs)
    if agent_exclude_logs is not None:
        config.agent.exclude_logs.extend(agent_exclude_logs)
    for mcp_config_path in mcp_config or []:
        config.agent.mcp_servers.extend(load_mcp_servers(mcp_config_path))
    if skills is not None:
        config.agent.skills.extend(skills)

    if environment_type is not None:
        warn_deprecated_flag("--environment-type", "--env")
        config.environment.type = environment_type
    if allow_environment_hosts is not None:
        config.environment.extra_allowed_hosts.extend(allow_environment_hosts)
    if environment_import_path is not None:
        warn_deprecated_flag("--environment-import-path", "--env")
        config.environment.import_path = environment_import_path
        config.environment.type = None  # Clear type so import_path takes precedence
    if environment is not None:
        env_type, env_import_path = resolve_environment_spec(environment)
        config.environment.type = env_type
        config.environment.import_path = env_import_path
    if environment_force_build is not None:
        config.environment.force_build = environment_force_build
    if environment_delete is not None:
        config.environment.delete = environment_delete
    if cpus is not None:
        config.environment.cpu_enforcement_policy = cpus
    if memory is not None:
        config.environment.memory_enforcement_policy = memory
    if override_cpus is not None:
        config.environment.override_cpus = override_cpus
    if override_memory_mb is not None:
        config.environment.override_memory_mb = override_memory_mb
    if override_storage_mb is not None:
        config.environment.override_storage_mb = override_storage_mb
    if override_gpus is not None:
        config.environment.override_gpus = override_gpus
    if override_tpu is not None:
        config.environment.override_tpu = parse_tpu_spec(override_tpu)
    if mounts is not None:
        config.environment.mounts = json.loads(mounts)
    if extra_docker_compose is not None:
        config.environment.extra_docker_compose.extend(extra_docker_compose)
    if environment_kwargs is not None:
        config.environment.kwargs.update(parse_kwargs(environment_kwargs))

    if verifier_timeout_sec is not None:
        config.verifier.override_timeout_sec = verifier_timeout_sec
    if verifier_env is not None:
        config.verifier.env.update(parse_env_vars(verifier_env))
    if verifier_include_logs is not None:
        config.verifier.include_logs.extend(verifier_include_logs)
    if verifier_exclude_logs is not None:
        config.verifier.exclude_logs.extend(verifier_exclude_logs)
    if verifier_import_path is not None:
        warn_deprecated_flag("--verifier-import-path", "--verifier")
        config.verifier.import_path = verifier_import_path
    if verifier is not None:
        config.verifier.import_path = verifier
    if verifier_kwargs is not None:
        config.verifier.kwargs.update(parse_kwargs(verifier_kwargs))

    if task_git_url is not None:
        config.task = TaskConfig(
            path=path or config.task.path,
            git_url=task_git_url,
            git_commit_id=task_git_commit_id,
        )
    elif path is not None:
        config.task = TaskConfig(path=path)

    # Single event loop for create + run so any singletons (httpx pools,
    # background refresh tasks, etc.) bound to the loop survive across the
    # whole trial lifecycle instead of being stranded when `Trial.create`'s
    # loop closes.
    async def _create_and_run() -> TrialResult:
        trial = await Trial.create(config)

        console.print(f"Starting trial: {config.trial_name}")
        console.print(f"Task: {config.task.get_task_id().get_name()}")
        agent_display = config.agent.name or config.agent.import_path or "unknown"
        console.print(f"Agent: {agent_display}")
        environment_display = config.environment.import_path or (
            config.environment.type.value if config.environment.type else "unknown"
        )
        console.print(f"Environment: {environment_display}")
        console.print(f"Trials directory: {config.trials_dir}")

        return await trial.run()

    result = run_async(_create_and_run())

    console.print("\n[bold green]Trial completed![/bold green]")
    console.print(f"Trial name: {result.trial_name}")
    console.print(f"Task: {result.task_name}")
    console.print(f"Started: {result.started_at}")
    console.print(f"Finished: {result.finished_at}")

    if result.exception_info:
        console.print(
            f"[bold red]Error: {result.exception_info.exception_type}[/bold red]"
        )
        console.print(f"Message: {result.exception_info.exception_message}")
    elif result.verifier_result:
        console.print(f"Rewards: {result.verifier_result.rewards}")
    else:
        console.print("Trial completed with no errors and no verifier result")

    return result


@trials_app.command()
def summarize(
    trial_path: Annotated[Path, Argument(help="Path to trial directory.")] = Path("."),
):
    """Removed command shim."""
    console.print(
        "[red]Error: 'harbor trials summarize' has been removed. "
        "Use 'harbor analyze <trial-dir>' instead.[/red]"
    )
    raise SystemExit(1)


def _humanize_bytes(n: int) -> str:
    size: float = float(n)
    for unit in ("B", "KB", "MB", "GB"):
        if size < 1024:
            return f"{size:.1f} {unit}" if unit != "B" else f"{int(size)} {unit}"
        size /= 1024
    return f"{size:.1f} TB"


@trials_app.command()
def download(
    trial_id: Annotated[str, Argument(help="Trial ID (UUID) to download.")],
    output_dir: Annotated[
        Path,
        Option(
            "--output-dir",
            "-o",
            help="Directory in which to materialize the trial_dir. "
            "Defaults to ./trials.",
        ),
    ] = Path("trials"),
    overwrite: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option("--overwrite", help="Replace an existing trial_dir if present."),
    ] = False,
    debug: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option("--debug", help="Show extra details on failure.", hidden=True),
    ] = False,
) -> None:
    """Download a single trial from the Harbor platform."""
    from uuid import UUID

    from typer import echo

    try:
        trial_uuid = UUID(trial_id)
    except ValueError:
        echo(f"Error: {trial_id!r} is not a valid UUID.")
        raise SystemExit(1) from None

    async def _download() -> None:
        from harbor.download.downloader import Downloader

        downloader = Downloader()

        try:
            await downloader.db.get_user_id()
        except RuntimeError as exc:
            echo(str(exc))
            raise SystemExit(1) from None

        # See `harbor job download` for why we don't `.resolve()` here.
        with console.status(f"[cyan]Downloading trial {trial_uuid}..."):
            result = await downloader.download_trial(
                trial_uuid, output_dir, overwrite=overwrite
            )

        echo(
            f"Downloaded {result.trial_name} "
            f"({_humanize_bytes(result.archive_size_bytes)}) "
            f"in {result.download_time_sec:.2f}s → {result.output_dir}"
        )
        echo(f"Analyze trajectory by running `harbor analyze {result.output_dir}`")

    try:
        run_async(_download())
    except SystemExit:
        raise
    except Exception as exc:
        echo(f"Error: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
        if debug:
            raise
        raise SystemExit(1) from None

483	def _sanitize_task_name(name: str) -> tuple[str, list[str]]:
484	    """Sanitize a task name by removing invalid characters.
485	
486	    Returns the sanitized name and a list of removed characters.
487	    """
488	    import re
489	
490	    removed = sorted(set(c for c in name if not re.match(r"[a-zA-Z0-9._-]", c)))
491	    sanitized = re.sub(r"[^a-zA-Z0-9._-]", "", name)
492	    # Ensure it doesn't start with a dot
493	    sanitized = sanitized.lstrip(".")
494	    return sanitized, removed

497	def _update_single_task(
498	    task_dir: Path,
499	    org: str,
500	    authors: list[Author],
501	    keywords: list[str],
502	    description: str = "",
503	    overwrite: bool = False,
504	) -> str | None:
505	    """Update a single task's package info. Returns the package name, or None if skipped."""
506	    from harbor.models.task.config import PackageInfo
507	
508	    task_name = task_dir.name
509	    sanitized_name, removed_chars = _sanitize_task_name(task_name)
510	    if removed_chars:
511	        console.print(
512	            f"[yellow]Warning: Removed invalid characters {removed_chars} "
513	            f"from task name '{task_name}' -> '{sanitized_name}'[/yellow]"
514	        )
515	    if not sanitized_name:
516	        raise ValueError(
517	            f"Task name '{task_name}' contains no valid characters after sanitization"
518	        )
519	    package_name = f"{org}/{sanitized_name}"
520	
521	    paths = TaskPaths(task_dir)
522	    config = TaskConfig.model_validate_toml(paths.config_path.read_text())
523	    if config.task is not None and not overwrite:
524	        return None
525	
526	    package_info = PackageInfo(
527	        name=package_name,
528	        description=description,
529	        authors=authors,
530	        keywords=keywords,
531	    )
532	
533	    config.task = package_info
534	    config.schema_version = "1.3"
535	    paths.config_path.write_text(config.model_dump_toml())
536	    return package_name

539	def _parse_authors(author: list[str] | None) -> list[Author]:
540	    """Parse author strings into Author objects.
541	
542	    Delegates to harbor.cli.init._parse_authors.
543	    """
544	    from harbor.cli.init import _parse_authors as _do_parse
545	
546	    return _do_parse(author)

549	@tasks_app.command()
550	def update(
551	    folders: Annotated[
552	        list[Path],
553	        Argument(
554	            help="Path(s) to task directory, or parent directories when using --scan."
555	        ),
556	    ],
557	    org: Annotated[
558	        str,
559	        Option(
560	            "--org", help="Organization name for the task package (e.g., 'harbor')."
561	        ),
562	    ],
563	    scan: Annotated[
564	        bool,
565	        Option(
566	            "--scan",
567	            help="Treat folders as directories of tasks and update all of them.",
568	        ),
569	    ] = False,
570	    description: Annotated[
571	        str,
572	        Option("-d", "--description", help="Human-readable description of the task."),
573	    ] = "",
574	    author: Annotated[
575	        list[str] | None,
576	        Option(
577	            "--author",
578	            help="Author in 'Name <email>' or 'Name' format. Can be used multiple times.",
579	        ),
580	    ] = None,
581	    keyword: Annotated[
582	        list[str] | None,
583	        Option(
584	            "--keyword",
585	            help="Keyword for search and categorization. Can be used multiple times.",
586	        ),
587	    ] = None,
588	    overwrite: Annotated[
589	        bool,
590	        Option(
591	            "--overwrite",
592	            help="Overwrite existing [task] section. By default, tasks with existing package info are skipped.",
593	        ),
594	    ] = False,
595	):
596	    """Add or update task package info in task.toml."""
597	    authors = _parse_authors(author)
598	    keywords = keyword or []
599	
600	    # Collect all task dirs to process
601	    task_dirs: list[Path] = []
602	    for folder in folders:
603	        folder = folder.resolve()
604	        if not folder.exists():
605	            console.print(f"[red]Error: folder does not exist: {folder}[/red]")
606	            raise SystemExit(1)
607	
608	        if scan:
609	            discovered = sorted(
610	                d for d in folder.iterdir() if d.is_dir() and (d / "task.toml").exists()
611	            )
612	            task_dirs.extend(discovered)
613	        else:
614	            if not (folder / "task.toml").exists():
615	                console.print(f"[red]Error: task.toml not found in {folder}[/red]")
616	                raise SystemExit(1)
617	            task_dirs.append(folder)
618	
619	    if not task_dirs:
620	        console.print("[yellow]No tasks found.[/yellow]")
621	        return
622	
623	    updated = 0
624	    skipped = 0
625	    failed = 0
626	    for task_dir in task_dirs:
627	        try:
628	            pkg = _update_single_task(
629	                task_dir,
630	                org,
631	                authors,
632	                keywords,
633	                description=description,
634	                overwrite=overwrite,
635	            )
636	            if pkg is None:
637	                skipped += 1
638	            else:
639	                console.print(f"[green]Updated: {pkg}[/green]")
640	                updated += 1
641	        except Exception as e:
642	            console.print(f"[red]Failed {task_dir.name}: {e}[/red]")
643	            failed += 1
644	
645	    console.print(
646	        f"\n[blue]Updated {updated} task(s), {skipped} skipped, {failed} failed.[/blue]"
647	    )

754	    from harbor.cli.annotator.annotator import Annotator

756	
757	    task_dirs: list[Path] = []

768	            )
769	            task_dirs.extend(discovered)

772	                console.print(f"[red]Error: task.toml not found in {p}[/red]")

779

781

784	            task_dirs=task_dirs,

786	            agent=agent,
787	            model=model,
788	            environment=environment,

795	            config_path=config,

799	        result = run_async(annotator.run())
800	    except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError, RuntimeError) as e:
801	        console.print(f"[red]Error: {e}[/red]")
802	        raise SystemExit(1)

807	    )

809	        console.print(f"[bold]Job:[/bold] {result.job_dir}")
810	    for error in result.errors:
811	        console.print(f"[red]  {error}[/red]")
812	    if result.failed:
813	        raise SystemExit(1)

986	                )

import sys
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Annotated, Any
from uuid import uuid4

from rich.console import Console
from rich.table import Table
from typer import Argument, Option, Typer

from harbor.cli.init import _init_task, _resolve_name
from harbor.cli.utils import run_async
from harbor.mappers.terminal_bench import TerminalBenchMapper
from harbor.models.environment_type import EnvironmentType
from harbor.models.task.config import Author, TaskConfig
from harbor.models.task.paths import TaskPaths
from harbor.models.task.task import Task
from harbor.models.trial.paths import EnvironmentPaths, TrialPaths

tasks_app = Typer(
    no_args_is_help=True, context_settings={"help_option_names": ["-h", "--help"]}
)

console = Console()


def render_result_table(title: str, rows: list[tuple[str, str, str]]):
    table = Table(title=title, show_lines=True)
    table.add_column("Check")
    table.add_column("Outcome")
    table.add_column("Explanation")

    style_map = {
        "PASS": "green",
        "FAIL": "red",
        "NOT_APPLICABLE": "grey50",
        "pass": "green",
        "fail": "red",
        "not_applicable": "grey50",
    }

    for check, outcome, explanation in rows:
        table.add_row(
            check, outcome, explanation, style=style_map.get(outcome, "white")
        )

    console.print(table)


@tasks_app.command()
def init(
    name: Annotated[str | None, Argument(help="Task name (org/name).")] = None,
    tasks_dir: Annotated[
        Path, Option("-p", "--tasks-dir", help="Path to tasks directory.")
    ] = Path("."),
    org: Annotated[str | None, Option("--org", help="Organization name.")] = None,
    description: Annotated[str, Option("--description", help="Task description.")] = "",
    no_pytest: Annotated[
        bool, Option("--no-pytest", help="Do not include pytest test template.")
    ] = False,
    no_solution: Annotated[
        bool, Option("--no-solution", help="Do not include solution template.")
    ] = False,
    include_canary_strings: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--include-canary-strings", help="Include canary strings in the task files."
        ),
    ] = False,
    metadata_template: Annotated[
        Path | None,
        Option(
            "--metadata-template",
            help="Path to a template TOML file for task metadata and defaults.",
        ),
    ] = None,
    include_standard_metadata: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--include-standard-metadata",
            help="Deprecated. Use --metadata-template instead.",
            hidden=True,
        ),
    ] = False,
    no_package: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option("--no-package", help="Do not include [task] section in task.toml."),
    ] = False,
    author: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--author",
            help="Author in 'Name <email>' or 'Name' format. Can be used multiple times.",
        ),
    ] = None,
    steps: Annotated[
        int,
        Option(
            "--steps",
            help="Scaffold a multi-step task with N steps. 0 (default) creates a single-step task.",
            min=0,
        ),
    ] = 0,
):
    """Initialize a new task directory."""
    from harbor.cli.init import _parse_authors

    if include_standard_metadata:
        console.print(
            "[red]Error: --include-standard-metadata has been removed. "
            "Use --metadata-template <path-to-template.toml> instead.[/red]"
        )
        raise SystemExit(1)

    if metadata_template is not None and not metadata_template.exists():
        console.print(f"[red]Error: Template file not found: {metadata_template}[/red]")
        raise SystemExit(1)

    resolved_name = _resolve_name(name, org)
    _init_task(
        name=resolved_name,
        output_dir=tasks_dir,
        no_pytest=no_pytest,
        no_solution=no_solution,
        include_canary_strings=include_canary_strings,
        no_package=no_package,
        description=description,
        authors=_parse_authors(author),
        metadata_template=metadata_template,
        steps=steps,
    )


@tasks_app.command()
def download(
    name: Annotated[
        str,
        Argument(
            help="Task to download in 'org/name@ref' format (defaults to @latest)."
        ),
    ],
    output_dir: Annotated[
        Path | None,
        Option(
            "-o",
            "--output-dir",
            help="Directory to download to. Defaults to current directory in export mode, or ~/.cache/harbor/tasks in cache mode.",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    overwrite: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--overwrite",
            help="Overwrite existing tasks.",
        ),
    ] = False,
    export: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--export",
            help="Export mode (default): <output-dir>/<task-name>/. Defaults output dir to current directory.",
        ),
    ] = False,
    cache: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--cache",
            help="Cache mode: content-addressable layout under ~/.cache/harbor/tasks.",
        ),
    ] = False,
):
    """Download a task from the Harbor package registry.

    Examples:
        harbor task download org/my-task
        harbor task download org/my-task@latest
        harbor task download org/my-task@3
    """
    from harbor.cli.download import _download_task, _resolve_export_mode

    export_mode = _resolve_export_mode(export, cache)

    # Default to export mode
    if export_mode is None:
        export_mode = True

    if export_mode and output_dir is None:
        output_dir = Path(".")

    if "@" in name:
        bare_name, ref = name.split("@", 1)
    else:
        bare_name = name
        ref = "latest"

    if "/" not in bare_name:
        console.print("[red]Error: Task name must be in 'org/name' format.[/red]")
        raise SystemExit(1)

    run_async(
        _download_task(
            name=bare_name,
            ref=ref,
            overwrite=overwrite,
            output_dir=output_dir,
            export=export_mode,
        )
    )


@tasks_app.command()
def start_env(
    path: Annotated[
        Path,
        Option(
            "-p",
            "--path",
            help="Path to task directory.",
            rich_help_panel="Task",
        ),
    ],
    environment_type: Annotated[
        EnvironmentType,
        Option(
            "-e",
            "--env",
            help="Environment type to start.",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
        ),
    ] = EnvironmentType.DOCKER,
    environment_import_path: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--environment-import-path",
            help="Import path for custom environment (module.path:ClassName).",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    mounts: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--mounts",
            "--mounts-json",
            help="JSON array of volume mounts for the environment container "
            "(Docker Compose service volume format). --mounts-json is a "
            "deprecated alias.",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    environment_kwargs: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--ek",
            "--environment-kwarg",
            help="Environment kwarg in key=value format (can be used multiple times).",
            rich_help_panel="Environment",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    all: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "-a",
            "--all",
            help="Add solution and tests to environment.",
            rich_help_panel="Setup",
        ),
    ] = True,
    interactive: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "-i",
            "--interactive/--non-interactive",
            help="Start environment in interactive mode.",
            rich_help_panel="Setup",
        ),
    ] = True,
    agent_name: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--agent",
            help="Agent name to install in the environment.",
            rich_help_panel="Agent",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    agent_import_path: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--agent-import-path",
            help="Import path for custom agent (module.path:ClassName).",
            rich_help_panel="Agent",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    model_name: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "-m",
            "--model",
            help="Model name for the agent (e.g., 'anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022').",
            rich_help_panel="Agent",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    agent_kwargs: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--ak",
            "--agent-kwarg",
            help="Additional agent kwarg in the format 'key=value'. Can be set multiple times.",
            rich_help_panel="Agent",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
):
    """Start an environment for a task."""
    import json

    from harbor.agents.factory import AgentFactory
    from harbor.environments.factory import EnvironmentFactory
    from harbor.models.trial.config import AgentConfig

    def parse_kwargs(kwargs_list: list[str] | None) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Parse key=value strings into a dictionary."""
        if not kwargs_list:
            return {}
        result = {}
        for kwarg in kwargs_list:
            if "=" not in kwarg:
                raise ValueError(f"Invalid kwarg format: {kwarg}. Expected key=value")
            key, value = kwarg.split("=", 1)
            key = key.strip()
            value = value.strip()
            try:
                result[key] = json.loads(value)
            except json.JSONDecodeError:
                result[key] = value
        return result

    task = Task(path)

    # Create agent config if agent is specified
    agent = None
    agent_config = None
    if agent_name is not None or agent_import_path is not None:
        agent_config = AgentConfig(
            name=agent_name,
            import_path=agent_import_path,
            model_name=model_name,
            kwargs=parse_kwargs(agent_kwargs),
        )

    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as temp_trial_dir:
        trial_paths = TrialPaths(trial_dir=Path(temp_trial_dir))
        trial_paths.mkdir()

        # On Windows, tempfile creates directories with owner-only ACLs
        # that prevent Docker Desktop's Linux VM from accessing bind
        # mounts.  Grant Everyone access so the mount succeeds.
        if sys.platform == "win32":
            import subprocess

            subprocess.run(
                [
                    "icacls",
                    temp_trial_dir,
                    "/grant",
                    "Everyone:(OI)(CI)F",
                    "/T",
                    "/Q",
                ],
                check=True,
                capture_output=True,
            )

        extra_env_kwargs = parse_kwargs(environment_kwargs)
        if mounts is not None:
            extra_env_kwargs["mounts"] = json.loads(mounts)

        if environment_import_path is not None:
            environment = EnvironmentFactory.create_environment_from_import_path(
                environment_import_path,
                environment_dir=task.paths.environment_dir,
                environment_name=task.short_name,
                session_id=str(uuid4()),
                trial_paths=trial_paths,
                task_env_config=task.config.environment,
                **extra_env_kwargs,
            )
        else:
            environment = EnvironmentFactory.create_environment(
                environment_type,
                environment_dir=task.paths.environment_dir,
                environment_name=task.short_name,
                session_id=str(uuid4()),
                trial_paths=trial_paths,
                task_env_config=task.config.environment,
                **extra_env_kwargs,
            )
        if environment.capabilities.mounted:
            trial_paths.chmod_dir()

        if agent_config is not None:
            agent = AgentFactory.create_agent_from_config(
                agent_config,
                logs_dir=trial_paths.agent_dir,
            )

        async def main():
            try:
                await environment.start(force_build=True)

                if all:
                    env_paths = EnvironmentPaths.for_os(environment.os)
                    await environment.upload_dir(
                        task.paths.solution_dir,
                        str(env_paths.solution_dir),
                    )
                    await environment.upload_dir(
                        task.paths.tests_dir,
                        str(env_paths.tests_dir),
                    )

                if task.config.environment.healthcheck is not None:
                    console.print("[blue]Running healthcheck...[/blue]")
                    await environment.run_healthcheck()
                    console.print("[green]✓ Healthcheck passed[/green]")

                if agent is not None:
                    console.print("[blue]Setting up agent in environment...[/blue]")
                    environment.default_user = task.config.agent.user
                    await agent.setup(environment=environment)
                    console.print("[green]✓ Agent setup complete[/green]")

                if interactive:
                    try:
                        await environment.attach()
                    except NotImplementedError as e:
                        console.print(f"[red]❌ {e}[/red]")
            finally:
                await environment.stop(delete=True)

        run_async(main())


# `debug` and `check` were removed as task subcommands, but are kept as hidden
# commands so that the old invocations still surface a helpful migration message
# instead of Typer's generic "No such command". They are hidden so that they no
# longer appear in `harbor task --help` advertising commands that no longer work.
@tasks_app.command(hidden=True)
def debug(
    task_id: Annotated[str, Argument(help="Task ID to analyze.")] = "",
    model_name: Annotated[
        str, Option("-m", "--model", help="The name of the model to use.")
    ] = "",
):
    """Debug task failures and analyze instruction sufficiency."""
    console.print(
        "[red]Error: 'harbor task debug' has been removed. "
        "Use 'harbor analyze <trial-dir|job-dir>' instead.[/red]"
    )
    raise SystemExit(1)


@tasks_app.command(hidden=True)
def check(
    task: Annotated[Path, Argument(help="Task name or path to task directory.")] = Path(
        "."
    ),
):
    """Run quality checks on a task definition."""
    console.print(
        "[red]Error: 'harbor task check' has been removed. "
        "Use 'harbor check <task-dir>' instead.[/red]"
    )
    raise SystemExit(1)


def _sanitize_task_name(name: str) -> tuple[str, list[str]]:
    """Sanitize a task name by removing invalid characters.

    Returns the sanitized name and a list of removed characters.
    """
    import re

    removed = sorted(set(c for c in name if not re.match(r"[a-zA-Z0-9._-]", c)))
    sanitized = re.sub(r"[^a-zA-Z0-9._-]", "", name)
    # Ensure it doesn't start with a dot
    sanitized = sanitized.lstrip(".")
    return sanitized, removed


def _update_single_task(
    task_dir: Path,
    org: str,
    authors: list[Author],
    keywords: list[str],
    description: str = "",
    overwrite: bool = False,
) -> str | None:
    """Update a single task's package info. Returns the package name, or None if skipped."""
    from harbor.models.task.config import PackageInfo

    task_name = task_dir.name
    sanitized_name, removed_chars = _sanitize_task_name(task_name)
    if removed_chars:
        console.print(
            f"[yellow]Warning: Removed invalid characters {removed_chars} "
            f"from task name '{task_name}' -> '{sanitized_name}'[/yellow]"
        )
    if not sanitized_name:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Task name '{task_name}' contains no valid characters after sanitization"
        )
    package_name = f"{org}/{sanitized_name}"

    paths = TaskPaths(task_dir)
    config = TaskConfig.model_validate_toml(paths.config_path.read_text())
    if config.task is not None and not overwrite:
        return None

    package_info = PackageInfo(
        name=package_name,
        description=description,
        authors=authors,
        keywords=keywords,
    )

    config.task = package_info
    config.schema_version = "1.3"
    paths.config_path.write_text(config.model_dump_toml())
    return package_name


def _parse_authors(author: list[str] | None) -> list[Author]:
    """Parse author strings into Author objects.

    Delegates to harbor.cli.init._parse_authors.
    """
    from harbor.cli.init import _parse_authors as _do_parse

    return _do_parse(author)


@tasks_app.command()
def update(
    folders: Annotated[
        list[Path],
        Argument(
            help="Path(s) to task directory, or parent directories when using --scan."
        ),
    ],
    org: Annotated[
        str,
        Option(
            "--org", help="Organization name for the task package (e.g., 'harbor')."
        ),
    ],
    scan: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--scan",
            help="Treat folders as directories of tasks and update all of them.",
        ),
    ] = False,
    description: Annotated[
        str,
        Option("-d", "--description", help="Human-readable description of the task."),
    ] = "",
    author: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--author",
            help="Author in 'Name <email>' or 'Name' format. Can be used multiple times.",
        ),
    ] = None,
    keyword: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--keyword",
            help="Keyword for search and categorization. Can be used multiple times.",
        ),
    ] = None,
    overwrite: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--overwrite",
            help="Overwrite existing [task] section. By default, tasks with existing package info are skipped.",
        ),
    ] = False,
):
    """Add or update task package info in task.toml."""
    authors = _parse_authors(author)
    keywords = keyword or []

    # Collect all task dirs to process
    task_dirs: list[Path] = []
    for folder in folders:
        folder = folder.resolve()
        if not folder.exists():
            console.print(f"[red]Error: folder does not exist: {folder}[/red]")
            raise SystemExit(1)

        if scan:
            discovered = sorted(
                d for d in folder.iterdir() if d.is_dir() and (d / "task.toml").exists()
            )
            task_dirs.extend(discovered)
        else:
            if not (folder / "task.toml").exists():
                console.print(f"[red]Error: task.toml not found in {folder}[/red]")
                raise SystemExit(1)
            task_dirs.append(folder)

    if not task_dirs:
        console.print("[yellow]No tasks found.[/yellow]")
        return

    updated = 0
    skipped = 0
    failed = 0
    for task_dir in task_dirs:
        try:
            pkg = _update_single_task(
                task_dir,
                org,
                authors,
                keywords,
                description=description,
                overwrite=overwrite,
            )
            if pkg is None:
                skipped += 1
            else:
                console.print(f"[green]Updated: {pkg}[/green]")
                updated += 1
        except Exception as e:
            console.print(f"[red]Failed {task_dir.name}: {e}[/red]")
            failed += 1

    console.print(
        f"\n[blue]Updated {updated} task(s), {skipped} skipped, {failed} failed.[/blue]"
    )


@tasks_app.command()
def annotate(
    paths: Annotated[
        list[Path],
        Argument(
            help="Task directory path(s), or parent directories when using --scan."
        ),
    ],
    scan: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--scan",
            help="Treat paths as parent directories and discover task subdirectories.",
        ),
    ] = False,
    n_concurrent: Annotated[
        int,
        Option(
            "-n",
            "--n-concurrent",
            help="Maximum number of concurrent annotation trials.",
        ),
    ] = 4,
    agent: Annotated[
        str,
        Option("-a", "--agent", help="Agent to use for annotation."),
    ] = "claude-code",
    model: Annotated[
        str,
        Option(
            "-m",
            "--model",
            help="Model to use for annotation.",
        ),
    ] = "claude-sonnet-4-6",
    agent_kwargs: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--ak",
            "--agent-kwarg",
            help="Agent kwarg key=value (repeatable).",
        ),
    ] = None,
    agent_env: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--ae",
            "--agent-env",
            help="Env var KEY=VALUE for the agent (repeatable).",
        ),
    ] = None,
    environment: Annotated[
        EnvironmentType,
        Option(
            "-e",
            "--env",
            help="Environment type to run annotation in.",
        ),
    ] = EnvironmentType.DOCKER,
    environment_kwargs: Annotated[
        list[str] | None,
        Option(
            "--ek",
            "--environment-kwarg",
            help="Environment kwarg key=value (repeatable).",
        ),
    ] = None,
    n_attempts: Annotated[
        int,
        Option("-k", "--n-attempts", help="Attempts per task."),
    ] = 1,
    job_name: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option("--job-name", help="Job name (default: timestamp)."),
    ] = None,
    jobs_dir: Annotated[
        Path | None,
        Option(
            "-o",
            "--jobs-dir",
            help="Directory to store job results (default: jobs).",
        ),
    ] = None,
    config: Annotated[
        Path | None,
        Option(
            "-c",
            "--config",
            help="Base JobConfig (YAML/JSON) for advanced settings.",
        ),
    ] = None,
    quiet: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option("-q", "--quiet", help="Suppress trial progress."),
    ] = False,
    overwrite: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--overwrite",
            help="Regenerate README.md even if it already exists.",
        ),
    ] = False,
):
    """Generate README.md and description for task(s) using a Harbor job."""
    from harbor.cli.annotator.annotator import Annotator
    from harbor.cli.utils import parse_env_vars, parse_kwargs

    task_dirs: list[Path] = []

    for p in paths:
        p = p.resolve()
        if not p.exists():
            console.print(f"[red]Error: path does not exist: {p}[/red]")
            raise SystemExit(1)

        if scan:
            discovered = sorted(
                d for d in p.iterdir() if d.is_dir() and (d / "task.toml").exists()
            )
            task_dirs.extend(discovered)
        else:
            if not (p / "task.toml").exists():
                console.print(f"[red]Error: task.toml not found in {p}[/red]")
                raise SystemExit(1)
            task_dirs.append(p)

    if not task_dirs:
        console.print("[yellow]No task directories found.[/yellow]")
        return

    console.print(f"[blue]Annotating {len(task_dirs)} task(s)...[/blue]")

    try:
        annotator = Annotator(
            task_dirs=task_dirs,
            n_concurrent=n_concurrent,
            agent=agent,
            model=model,
            environment=environment,
            n_attempts=n_attempts,
            job_name=job_name,
            jobs_dir=jobs_dir,
            agent_kwargs=parse_kwargs(agent_kwargs),
            agent_env=parse_env_vars(agent_env),
            environment_kwargs=parse_kwargs(environment_kwargs),
            config_path=config,
            quiet=quiet,
            overwrite=overwrite,
        )
        result = run_async(annotator.run())
    except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError, RuntimeError) as e:
        console.print(f"[red]Error: {e}[/red]")
        raise SystemExit(1)

    console.print(
        f"\n[blue]Done: {result.annotated} annotated, "
        f"{result.skipped} skipped, {result.failed} failed.[/blue]"
    )
    if result.job_dir is not None:
        console.print(f"[bold]Job:[/bold] {result.job_dir}")
    for error in result.errors:
        console.print(f"[red]  {error}[/red]")
    if result.failed:
        raise SystemExit(1)


@tasks_app.command()
def visibility(
    package: Annotated[
        str,
        Argument(
            help="Task package in 'org/name' format (e.g., 'harbor/hello-world')."
        ),
    ],
    public: Annotated[
        bool, Option("--public", help="Set visibility to public.")
    ] = False,
    private: Annotated[
        bool, Option("--private", help="Set visibility to private.")
    ] = False,
    toggle: Annotated[
        bool, Option("--toggle", help="Toggle between public and private.")
    ] = False,
):
    """Set or toggle the visibility of a published task."""
    from harbor.db.client import RegistryDB

    flags = sum([public, private, toggle])
    if flags == 0:
        console.print(
            "[red]Error: specify one of --public, --private, or --toggle.[/red]"
        )
        raise SystemExit(1)
    if flags > 1:
        console.print(
            "[red]Error: --public, --private, and --toggle are mutually exclusive.[/red]"
        )
        raise SystemExit(1)

    if "/" not in package:
        console.print("[red]Error: package must be in 'org/name' format.[/red]")
        raise SystemExit(1)

    org, name = package.split("/", 1)

    vis: str | None = None
    if public:
        vis = "public"
    elif private:
        vis = "private"

    async def _run() -> None:
        db = RegistryDB()
        try:
            await db.get_user_id()
        except RuntimeError as exc:
            console.print(f"[red]{exc}[/red]")
            raise SystemExit(1)

        result = await db.set_package_visibility(
            org=org,
            name=name,
            package_type="task",
            visibility=vis,
            toggle=toggle,
        )
        old = result.get("old_visibility", "unknown")
        new = result.get("new_visibility", "unknown")
        console.print(f"[green]Visibility changed: {old} → {new}[/green]")

    run_async(_run())


@tasks_app.command()
def migrate(
    input_path: Annotated[
        Path,
        Option(
            "-i",
            "--input",
            help="Path to Terminal Bench task directory or parent directory containing tasks.",
        ),
    ],
    output_path: Annotated[
        Path,
        Option(
            "-o",
            "--output",
            help="Path to output directory for Harbor format tasks.",
        ),
    ],
    cpus: Annotated[
        int | None,
        Option(
            "--cpus",
            help="Override CPUs for all migrated tasks.",
        ),
    ] = None,
    memory_mb: Annotated[
        int | None,
        Option(
            "--memory-mb",
            help="Override memory (MB) for all migrated tasks.",
        ),
    ] = None,
    storage_mb: Annotated[
        int | None,
        Option(
            "--storage-mb",
            help="Override storage (MB) for all migrated tasks.",
        ),
    ] = None,
    gpus: Annotated[
        int | None,
        Option(
            "--gpus",
            help="Override GPUs for all migrated tasks.",
        ),
    ] = None,
):
    """Migrate Terminal Bench tasks to Harbor format.

    WARNING: This migration tool is not foolproof and may require manual adjustments.
    Please review the migrated tasks carefully before use.
    """
    console.print(
        "[yellow]⚠️  WARNING: This migration tool is not foolproof. "
        "Please review migrated tasks carefully and make manual adjustments as needed.[/yellow]\n"
    )

    try:
        input_path = input_path.resolve()
        output_path = output_path.resolve()

        if not input_path.exists():
            console.print(f"[red]❌ Input path does not exist: {input_path}[/red]")
            return

        environment_overrides = {}
        if cpus is not None:
            environment_overrides["cpus"] = cpus
        if memory_mb is not None:
            environment_overrides["memory_mb"] = memory_mb
        if storage_mb is not None:
            environment_overrides["storage_mb"] = storage_mb
        if gpus is not None:
            environment_overrides["gpus"] = gpus

        mapper = TerminalBenchMapper(environment_overrides=environment_overrides)

        # Check if input_path is a single task or a directory of tasks
        if (input_path / "task.yaml").exists():
            # Single task migration
            console.print(f"[blue]📦 Migrating single task from: {input_path}[/blue]")
            task_name = input_path.name
            target_dir = output_path / task_name

            try:
                mapped_task_path = mapper._map_task(input_path, target_dir)
                console.print(
                    f"[green]✓ Successfully migrated task to: {mapped_task_path}[/green]"
                )
            except Exception as e:
                console.print(f"[red]❌ Failed to migrate task: {e}[/red]")
                raise
        else:
            # Multiple tasks migration
            console.print(f"[blue]📦 Migrating tasks from: {input_path}[/blue]")
            console.print(f"[blue]📤 Output directory: {output_path}[/blue]\n")

            result = mapper.map(input_path, output_path)

            console.print()
            if result.mapped:
                console.print(
                    f"[green]✓ Successfully migrated {len(result.mapped)} task(s)[/green]"
                )
            if result.failed:
                console.print(
                    f"[red]✗ Failed to migrate {len(result.failed)} task(s)[/red]"
                )
            if not result.mapped and not result.failed:
                console.print("[yellow]⚠️  No tasks found to migrate[/yellow]")

    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"[red]❌ Migration failed: {e}[/red]")
        raise

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Annotated, Sized

from typer import Option, Typer

traces_app = Typer(
    no_args_is_help=True, context_settings={"help_option_names": ["-h", "--help"]}
)


@traces_app.command("export")
def export(
    path: Annotated[
        Path,
        Option(
            "--path",
            "-p",
            help="Path to a trial dir or a root containing trials recursively",
        ),
    ],
    recursive: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--recursive/--no-recursive",
            help="Search recursively for trials under path",
        ),
    ] = True,
    episodes: Annotated[
        str,
        Option(
            "--episodes",
            help="Export all episodes or only the last episode per trial (all|last)",
        ),
    ] = "all",
    to_sharegpt: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--sharegpt/--no-sharegpt",
            help="Also emit ShareGPT-formatted conversations column",
        ),
    ] = False,
    push: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--push/--no-push", help="Push dataset to Hugging Face Hub after export"
        ),
    ] = False,
    repo_id: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--repo",
            help="Target HF repo id (org/name) when --push is set",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    verbose: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option("--verbose/--no-verbose", help="Print discovery details for debugging"),
    ] = False,
    filter: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--filter",
            help="Filter trials by result: success|failure|all (default all)",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = None,
    subagents: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--subagents/--no-subagents",
            help="Export subagent traces",
        ),
    ] = True,
    instruction_metadata: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--instruction-metadata/--no-instruction-metadata",
            help="Include instruction text for each row when available",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = False,
    verifier_metadata: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--verifier-metadata/--no-verifier-metadata",
            help="Include verifier stdout/stderr blobs when available",
            show_default=False,
        ),
    ] = False,
):
    from harbor.utils.traces_utils import export_traces as _export_traces

    if push and not repo_id:
        raise ValueError("--push requires --repo <org/name>")

    if episodes not in ("all", "last"):
        raise ValueError("--episodes must be one of: all, last")

    if filter and filter not in ("all", "success", "failure"):
        raise ValueError("--filter must be one of: success, failure, all")

    ds = _export_traces(
        root=path,
        recursive=recursive,
        episodes=episodes,
        to_sharegpt=to_sharegpt,
        repo_id=repo_id,
        push=push,
        verbose=verbose,
        success_filter=(None if (not filter or filter == "all") else filter),
        export_subagents=subagents,
        include_instruction=instruction_metadata,
        include_verifier_output=verifier_metadata,
    )

    # Handle different return types based on export_subagents
    if isinstance(ds, dict):
        # Multiple datasets returned (main + subagents)
        main_ds = ds.get("main")  # type: ignore[call-overload]
        main_count = len(main_ds) if main_ds else 0  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
        subagent_info = ", ".join(
            [
                f"{k}: {len(v)} rows"
                for k, v in ds.items()
                if k != "main" and isinstance(v, Sized)
            ]
        )
        print(f"Exported {main_count} main rows from {path}")
        if subagent_info:
            print(f"Subagent traces: {subagent_info}")
    else:
        # Single dataset returned (main only)
        print(f"Exported {len(ds)} rows from {path}")
