1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import json
4	from pathlib import Path
5	from typing import Annotated
6	
7	try:
8	    import yaml
9	except Exception:  # optional dependency if not using YAML configs
10	    yaml = None  # type: ignore
11	
12	from typer import Option, Typer
13	
14	from harbor.cli.utils import run_async
15	from harbor.models.job.config import JobConfig
16	from harbor.models.trial.config import TaskConfig
17	
18	sweeps_app = Typer(
19	    no_args_is_help=True, context_settings={"help_option_names": ["-h", "--help"]}
20	)
21	
22	
23	@sweeps_app.command("run")
24	def run_sweeps(
25	    config_path: Annotated[
26	        Path, Option("-c", "--config", help="Job config file (json or yaml)")
27	    ],
28	    sweeps: Annotated[
29	        int,
30	        Option("--max-sweeps", help="Max number of sweeps to run"),
31	    ] = 3,
32	    trials_per_task: Annotated[
33	        int,
34	        Option("--trials-per-task", help="Trials per task per sweep"),
35	    ] = 2,
36	    add_hint: Annotated[
37	        str | None,
38	        Option("--hint", help="Optional generic hint string to pass to agent kwargs"),
39	    ] = None,
40	    hints_file: Annotated[
41	        Path | None,
42	        Option(
43	            "--hints-file",
44	            help="JSON file mapping task name -> hint string",
45	            show_default=False,
46	        ),
47	    ] = None,
48	    export_repo: Annotated[
49	        str | None,
50	        Option(
51	            "--export-repo",
52	            help="Repo to push DatasetDict with success/failure splits",
53	            show_default=False,
54	        ),
55	    ] = None,
56	    export_repo_success: Annotated[
57	        str | None,
58	        Option(
59	            "--export-repo-success",
60	            help="Repo to push ONLY successes when using --export-separate",
61	            show_default=False,
62	        ),
63	    ] = None,
64	    export_repo_failure: Annotated[
65	        str | None,
66	        Option(
67	            "--export-repo-failure",
68	            help="Repo to push ONLY failures when using --export-separate",
69	            show_default=False,
70	        ),
71	    ] = None,
72	    push: Annotated[
73	        bool,
74	        Option("--push/--no-push", help="Push exported datasets to HF Hub"),
75	    ] = False,
76	    export_splits: Annotated[
77	        bool,
78	        Option(
79	            "--export-splits/--export-separate",
80	            help="Push one repo with splits vs two separate repos",
81	        ),
82	    ] = True,
83	):
84	    """Run successive sweeps, dropping tasks with ≥1 success each sweep.
85	
86	    At the end, export traces for all sweeps as a DatasetDict with 'success' and 'failure' splits
87	    when --export-repo is provided and --push is set.
88	    """
89	    from harbor.job import Job
90	
91	    # Load base config
92	    if config_path.suffix == ".yaml":
93	        if yaml is None:
94	            raise ImportError("pyyaml is required to read YAML configs; install pyyaml")
95	        base_config = JobConfig.model_validate(yaml.safe_load(config_path.read_text()))
96	    elif config_path.suffix == ".json":
97	        base_config = JobConfig.model_validate_json(config_path.read_text())
98	    else:
99	        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported config file format: {config_path.suffix}")
100	
101	    # Apply sweep-specific overrides
102	    base_config.n_attempts = int(trials_per_task)
103	
104	    if add_hint:
105	        # Merge the hint into each agent's kwargs
106	        for ag in base_config.agents:
107	            ag.kwargs = {**(ag.kwargs or {}), "hint": add_hint}
108	
109	    remaining_tasks: list[TaskConfig] = base_config.tasks.copy()
110	    job_dirs: list[Path] = []
111	
112	    # Load per-task hints if provided
113	    hints_map: dict[str, str] = {}
114	    if hints_file:
115	        try:
116	            hints_map = json.loads(hints_file.read_text())
117	            if not isinstance(hints_map, dict):
118	                print("[sweeps] --hints-file must be a JSON object {task_name: hint}")
119	                hints_map = {}
120	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError) as e:
121	            print(f"[sweeps] Failed to read hints file: {e}")
122	            hints_map = {}
123	
124	    # Single event loop for the whole sweep. Previously each sweep iteration
125	    # called `run_async` and created a fresh event loop — any singletons
126	    # (supabase client, httpx pool) bound to the first loop were stranded
127	    # when it closed, causing intermittent "Event loop is closed" failures
128	    # in later iterations as soon as anything touched Supabase.
129	    async def _run_sweep_iteration(cfg: JobConfig) -> Path:
130	        j = await Job.create(cfg)
131	        await j.run()
132	        return j.job_dir
133	
134	    async def _run_all_sweeps() -> None:
135	        nonlocal remaining_tasks
136	        for sweep_idx in range(1, sweeps + 1):
137	            if not remaining_tasks:
138	                print("[sweeps] All tasks succeeded; stopping early.")
139	                break
140	
141	            succeeded_by_task: set[str] = set()
142	
143	            if hints_map:
144	                # Run one job per task to inject per-task hint into agent kwargs
145	                print(
146	                    f"[sweeps] Starting sweep {sweep_idx} with per-task hints; {len(remaining_tasks)} tasks"
147	                )
148	                for task in remaining_tasks:
149	                    task_name = task.get_task_id().get_name()
150	                    cfg = base_config.model_copy(deep=True)
151	                    cfg.tasks = [task]
152	                    cfg.job_name = (
153	                        f"{base_config.job_name}.sweep-{sweep_idx}.{task_name}"
154	                    )
155	                    hint_val = hints_map.get(task_name)
156	                    if hint_val:
157	                        for ag in cfg.agents:
158	                            ag.kwargs = {**(ag.kwargs or {}), "hint": hint_val}
159	
160	                    job_dir = await _run_sweep_iteration(cfg)
161	                    job_dirs.append(job_dir)
162	                    # Scan successes for this task
163	                    for trial_dir in job_dir.iterdir():
164	                        if not trial_dir.is_dir():
165	                            continue
166	                        rp = trial_dir / "result.json"
167	                        if not rp.exists():
168	                            continue
169	                        try:
170	                            data = rp.read_text()
171	                            obj = json.loads(data)
172	                            if not isinstance(obj, dict):
173	                                print(f"[sweeps] Ignoring non-object result in {rp}")
174	                                continue
175	                            vr = obj.get("verifier_result")
176	                            reward = vr.get("reward") if isinstance(vr, dict) else None
177	                            if reward is not None and float(reward) > 0.0:
178	                                succeeded_by_task.add(task_name)
179	                                break
180	                        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
181	                            print(f"[sweeps] JSON parse error in {rp}: {e}")
182	                        except (ValueError, OSError) as e:
183	                            # ValueError for float() casting; OSError for file read issues
184	                            print(f"[sweeps] Error processing {rp}: {e}")
185	            else:
186	                # Single job across all remaining tasks
187	                cfg = base_config.model_copy(deep=True)
188	                cfg.tasks = remaining_tasks
189	                cfg.job_name = f"{base_config.job_name}.sweep-{sweep_idx}"
190	                print(
191	                    f"[sweeps] Starting sweep {sweep_idx} with {len(cfg.tasks)} tasks, {cfg.n_attempts} trials/task"
192	                )
193	
194	                job_dir = await _run_sweep_iteration(cfg)
195	                job_dirs.append(job_dir)
196	                for trial_dir in job_dir.iterdir():
197	                    if not trial_dir.is_dir():
198	                        continue
199	                    result_path = trial_dir / "result.json"
200	                    if not result_path.exists():
201	                        continue
202	                    try:
203	                        data = result_path.read_text()
204	                        obj = json.loads(data)
205	                        if not isinstance(obj, dict):
206	                            print(
207	                                f"[sweeps] Ignoring non-object result in {result_path}"
208	                            )
209	                            continue
210	                        vr = obj.get("verifier_result")
211	                        reward = vr.get("reward") if isinstance(vr, dict) else None
212	                        if reward is not None and float(reward) > 0.0:
213	                            task_name = (
214	                                obj.get("task_name") or trial_dir.name.split("__", 1)[0]
215	                            )
216	                            succeeded_by_task.add(task_name)
217	                    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
218	                        print(f"[sweeps] JSON parse error in {result_path}: {e}")
219	                    except (ValueError, OSError) as e:
220	                        print(f"[sweeps] Error processing {result_path}: {e}")
221	
222	            # Filter remaining tasks for next sweep
223	            before = len(remaining_tasks)
224	            remaining_tasks = [
225	                t
226	                for t in remaining_tasks
227	                if t.get_task_id().get_name() not in succeeded_by_task
228	            ]
229	            print(
230	                f"[sweeps] Sweep {sweep_idx} complete. Tasks: {before} -> {len(remaining_tasks)} remaining"
231	            )
232	
233	    run_async(_run_all_sweeps())
234	
235	    # Export traces at the end
236	    # Merge all job dirs and export success/failure splits
237	    if push and (export_repo or (export_repo_success and export_repo_failure)):
238	        from datasets import Dataset, DatasetDict, concatenate_datasets
239	
240	        from harbor.utils.traces_utils import export_traces as _export_traces
241	
242	        print("[sweeps] Exporting success/failure splits to HF")
243	        # Export success split
244	        ds_success = None
245	        ds_failure = None
246	        for jd in job_dirs:
247	            ds_s = _export_traces(
248	                jd,
249	                recursive=True,
250	                episodes="last",
251	                to_sharegpt=False,
252	                push=False,
253	                verbose=False,
254	                success_filter="success",
255	            )
256	            ds_f = _export_traces(
257	                jd,
258	                recursive=True,
259	                episodes="last",
260	                to_sharegpt=False,
261	                push=False,
262	                verbose=False,
263	                success_filter="failure",
264	            )
265	            if ds_success is None:
266	                ds_success = ds_s
267	                ds_failure = ds_f
268	            else:
269	                assert isinstance(ds_success, Dataset)
270	                assert isinstance(ds_failure, Dataset)
271	                assert isinstance(ds_s, Dataset)
272	                assert isinstance(ds_f, Dataset)
273	                if len(ds_s) > 0:
274	                    ds_success = concatenate_datasets([ds_success, ds_s])
275	                if len(ds_f) > 0:
276	                    ds_failure = concatenate_datasets([ds_failure, ds_f])
277	        if ds_success is None:
278	            ds_success = Dataset.from_list([])
279	        if ds_failure is None:
280	            ds_failure = Dataset.from_list([])
281	        if export_splits:
282	            if not export_repo:
283	                raise ValueError("--export-splits requires --export-repo <org/name>")
284	            dd = DatasetDict({"success": ds_success, "failure": ds_failure})  # ty: ignore[no-matching-overload]
285	            dd.push_to_hub(export_repo)
286	            print(f"[sweeps] Pushed splits to {export_repo}")
287	        else:
288	            if not (export_repo_success and export_repo_failure):
289	                raise ValueError(
290	                    "--export-separate requires --export-repo-success and --export-repo-failure"
291	                )
292	            if len(ds_success) > 0:
293	                ds_success.push_to_hub(export_repo_success)  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
294	                print(f"[sweeps] Pushed successes to {export_repo_success}")
295	            else:
296	                print("[sweeps] No successes to push")
297	            if len(ds_failure) > 0:
298	                ds_failure.push_to_hub(export_repo_failure)  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
299	                print(f"[sweeps] Pushed failures to {export_repo_failure}")
300	            else:
301	                print("[sweeps] No failures to push")
302	    else:
303	        print("[sweeps] Skipping push; set --push and --export-repo to upload.")
304

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Annotated

from typer import Argument, Option, Typer

adapters_app = Typer(
    no_args_is_help=True, context_settings={"help_option_names": ["-h", "--help"]}
)


@adapters_app.command()
def init(
    adapter_id: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Argument(
            help=(
                "Adapter ID (lowercase, no spaces). Leave empty to derive from --name."
            ),
        ),
    ] = None,
    adapters_dir: Annotated[
        Path,
        Option(
            "--adapters-dir",
            help="Directory in which to create the adapter folder.",
        ),
    ] = Path("adapters"),
    name: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--name",
            "-n",
            help="Vanilla benchmark name (e.g., SWE-bench, MLEBench)",
        ),
    ] = None,
    description: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option("--description", "-d", help="One-line adapter description for README"),
    ] = None,
    source_url: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option("--source-url", help="Source repository or paper URL"),
    ] = None,
    license_name: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option("--license", help="Dataset/benchmark license (for README)"),
    ] = None,
) -> None:
    """Launch the rich interactive wizard to initialize a new adapter template."""
    from harbor.cli.adapter_wizard import AdapterWizard

    wizard = AdapterWizard(
        adapters_dir,
        name=name,
        adapter_id=adapter_id,
        description=description,
        source_url=source_url,
        license_name=license_name,
    )
    wizard.run()


@adapters_app.command()
def review(
    path: Annotated[
        Path,
        Option(
            "--path",
            "-p",
            help="Path to the adapter directory to review.",
        ),
    ],
    agent: Annotated[
        str,
        Option(
            "--agent",
            "-a",
            help="Agent for AI review: 'claude' or 'codex'.",
        ),
    ] = "claude",
    skip_ai: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--skip-ai",
            help="Skip AI review, run only structural validation.",
        ),
    ] = False,
    model: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Option(
            "--model",
            "-m",
            help="Model for AI review. If not set, uses the agent's default model.",
        ),
    ] = None,
    original_fork_repo: Annotated[
        Path | None,
        Option(
            "--original-fork-repo",
            help="Path to the original benchmark fork repo for cross-review.",
        ),
    ] = None,
    output: Annotated[
        Path,
        Option(
            "--output",
            "-o",
            help="Save review to a Markdown file.",
        ),
    ] = Path("adapter-review-report.md"),
) -> None:
    """Review an adapter for structural compliance and code quality.

    Runs in up to three parts:

    1. Structural validation — checks required files, JSON schemas, canary strings,
       PR links, README sections, and cross-file consistency. No AI needed.

    2. AI review — uses an AI agent to perform a semantic code review.
       Supports --agent claude (local Claude Code) or --agent codex (local Codex CLI).
       Both run locally and read files from the adapter directory.
       Uses the agent's default model unless --model is specified.
       Use --skip-ai to run only structural validation.

    3. Original fork review (optional) — if --original-fork-repo is provided,
       reviews the forked benchmark repo for consistency with the adapter:
       fork existence/structure, README instructions, modifications, bug fixes,
       and alignment with Harbor parity settings.

    Results are saved to a Markdown report (default: adapter-review-report.md).
    """
    adapter_dir = Path(path)
    if not adapter_dir.is_dir():
        # Try resolving as adapter name under adapters/
        candidate = Path("adapters") / path
        if candidate.is_dir():
            adapter_dir = candidate
        else:
            print(f"Error: adapter directory not found: {path}")
            raise SystemExit(1)

    from harbor.cli.adapter_review import (
        run_ai_review,
        run_structural_validation,
        save_review,
    )

    print(f"=== Adapter Review: {adapter_dir.name} ===")

    # Part 1: Structural validation
    print("\n--- Part 1: Structural Validation ---")
    passed, structural_md = run_structural_validation(adapter_dir)

    # Part 2: AI review (includes fork review if --original-fork-repo provided)
    ai_md = ""
    fork_dir: Path | None = None
    if original_fork_repo is not None:
        fork_dir = Path(original_fork_repo)
        if not fork_dir.is_dir():
            print(f"Error: fork repo directory not found: {original_fork_repo}")
            raise SystemExit(1)

    if not skip_ai:
        print("\n--- Part 2: AI Review ---")
        ai_md = run_ai_review(adapter_dir, agent=agent, model=model, fork_dir=fork_dir)
    else:
        print("\n--- Part 2: AI Review (skipped) ---")

    # Save combined report
    save_review(
        adapter_dir.name, structural_md, ai_md, output, agent=agent, model=model
    )

    if not passed:
        raise SystemExit(1)

6	from rich.console import Console
7	from typer import Option, Typer
8	
9	cache_app = Typer(

16	def clean(
17	    force: Annotated[
18	        bool,
19	        Option(
20	            "-f",
21	            "--force",
22	            help="Skip confirmation prompt and clean immediately.",
23	        ),
24	    ] = False,
25	    dry: Annotated[
26	        bool,
27	        Option(
28	            "--dry",
29	            help="Show what would be cleaned without actually cleaning.",
30	        ),
31	    ] = False,
32	    no_docker: Annotated[
33	        bool,
34	        Option(
35	            "--no-docker",
36	            help="Skip Docker image cleanup.",
37	        ),
38	    ] = False,
39	    no_cache_dir: Annotated[
40	        bool,
41	        Option(
42	            "--no-cache-dir",
43	            help="Skip cache directory cleanup.",
44	        ),
45	    ] = False,
46	):
47	    """Clean Harbor cache: remove Docker images (alexgshaw/*, hb__*, sb__*) and ~/.cache/harbor folder."""
48	    cache_dir = Path("~/.cache/harbor").expanduser()
49	
50	    cache_exists = cache_dir.exists()
51	    cache_size = None
52	    if cache_exists and not no_cache_dir:
53	        total_size = sum(f.stat().st_size for f in cache_dir.rglob("*") if f.is_file())
54	        cache_size = total_size / (1024 * 1024)
55	
56	    docker_images = []
57	    if not no_docker:
58	        try:
59	            result = subprocess.run(
60	                ["docker", "images", "--format", "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}"],
61	                capture_output=True,
62	                text=True,
63	                check=True,
64	            )
65	            all_images = result.stdout.strip().split("\n")
66	            docker_images = [
67	                img
68	                for img in all_images
69	                if img.startswith("alexgshaw/")
70	                or img.startswith("hb__")
71	                or img.startswith("sb__")
72	            ]
73	        except subprocess.CalledProcessError:
74	            console.print("[yellow]⚠️  Could not list Docker images[/yellow]")
75	        except FileNotFoundError:
76	            console.print(
77	                "[yellow]⚠️  Docker not found, skipping image cleanup[/yellow]"
78	            )
79	
80	    console.print("\n[bold]Harbor Cache Cleanup[/bold]\n")
81	    if dry:
82	        console.print("[yellow]DRY RUN - No changes will be made[/yellow]\n")
83	
84	    if cache_exists and not no_cache_dir:
85	        console.print(f"[blue]Cache directory:[/blue] {cache_dir}")
86	        console.print(f"[blue]Cache size:[/blue] {cache_size:.2f} MB\n")
87	    elif not no_cache_dir:
88	        console.print(f"[dim]Cache directory not found: {cache_dir}[/dim]\n")
89	
90	    if docker_images:
91	        console.print(
92	            f"[blue]Docker images to remove:[/blue] {len(docker_images)} image(s)"
93	        )
94	        for img in docker_images[:10]:
95	            console.print(f"  • {img}")
96	        if len(docker_images) > 10:
97	            console.print(f"  ... and {len(docker_images) - 10} more")
98	        console.print()
99	    elif not no_docker:
100	        console.print("[dim]No matching Docker images found[/dim]\n")
101	
102	    should_clean_cache = cache_exists and not no_cache_dir
103	    should_clean_docker = bool(docker_images) and not no_docker
104	
105	    if not should_clean_cache and not should_clean_docker:
106	        console.print("[green]✓ Nothing to clean[/green]")
107	        return
108	
109	    if dry:
110	        console.print("[yellow]✓ Dry run completed (no changes made)[/yellow]")
111	        return
112	
113	    if not force:
114	        response = console.input("[yellow]Proceed with cleanup? (y/N):[/yellow] ")
115	        if response.lower() not in ["y", "yes"]:
116	            console.print("[dim]Cleanup cancelled[/dim]")
117	            return
118	
119	    cleaned = False
120	
121	    if should_clean_cache:
122	        try:
123	            shutil.rmtree(cache_dir)
124	            console.print(f"[green]✓ Removed cache directory: {cache_dir}[/green]")
125	            cleaned = True
126	        except Exception as e:
127	            console.print(f"[red]❌ Failed to remove cache directory: {e}[/red]")
128	
129	    if should_clean_docker:
130	        try:
131	            subprocess.run(
132	                ["docker", "rmi", "-f"] + docker_images,
133	                capture_output=True,
134	                text=True,
135	                check=True,
136	            )
137	            console.print(
138	                f"[green]✓ Removed {len(docker_images)} Docker image(s)[/green]"
139	            )
140	            cleaned = True
141	        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
142	            console.print(f"[red]❌ Failed to remove Docker images: {e.stderr}[/red]")
143	        except FileNotFoundError:
144	            console.print(
145	                "[yellow]⚠️  Docker not found, skipping image cleanup[/yellow]"
146	            )
147	
148	    if cleaned:
149	        console.print("\n[green bold]✓ Cache cleanup completed[/green bold]")
150	    else:
151	        console.print("\n[yellow]⚠️  No cleanup was performed[/yellow]")
152

1	"""CLI command for trajectory web viewer."""
2	
3	import os
4	import socket
5	import subprocess
6	from pathlib import Path
7	from typing import Annotated

9	from rich.console import Console
10	from typer import Argument, Option

12	console = Console(stderr=True)
13

15	STATIC_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "viewer" / "static"

18	VIEWER_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent.parent / "apps" / "viewer"

21	def _parse_port_range(port_str: str) -> tuple[int, int]:
22	    """Parse a port or port range string into (start, end) tuple."""
23	    if "-" in port_str:
24	        parts = port_str.split("-")
25	        return int(parts[0]), int(parts[1])
26	    port = int(port_str)
27	    return port, port

30	def _has_bun() -> bool:
31	    """Check if bun is available."""

34	    return shutil.which("bun") is not None

37	def _build_viewer() -> bool:
38	    """Build the viewer frontend and copy to static directory.
39	
40	    Returns True on success, False on failure.
41	    """
42	    if not VIEWER_DIR.exists():
43	        console.print(f"[red]Error:[/red] Viewer source not found at {VIEWER_DIR}")
44	        return False
45	
46	    if not _has_bun():
47	        console.print(
48	            "[red]Error:[/red] bun is required to build the viewer. "
49	            "Install it from https://bun.com"
50	        )
51	        return False
52	
53	    console.print("[blue]Building viewer...[/blue]")
54	
55	    # Install dependencies
56	    console.print("  Installing dependencies...")
57	    result = subprocess.run(
58	        ["bun", "install"],

60	        capture_output=True,
61	        text=True,
62	    )
63	    if result.returncode != 0:
64	        console.print("[red]Error:[/red] Failed to install dependencies")
65	        console.print(result.stderr)
66	        return False
67	
68	    # Build
69	    console.print("  Building frontend...")
70	    result = subprocess.run(
71	        ["bun", "run", "build"],

74	        text=True,
75	    )
76	    if result.returncode != 0:
77	        console.print("[red]Error:[/red] Build failed")

79	        return False
80	
81	    # Copy build output to static directory
82	    build_client_dir = VIEWER_DIR / "build" / "client"

85	        return False
86	
87	    import shutil
88	
89	    # Remove existing static dir if present
90	    if STATIC_DIR.exists():
91	        shutil.rmtree(STATIC_DIR)
92	
93	    # Copy build output
94	    shutil.copytree(build_client_dir, STATIC_DIR)
95	    console.print("[green]Build complete![/green]")
96	    return True
97	
98	
99	def _find_available_port(host: str, start: int, end: int) -> int | None:
100	    """Find an available port in the given range."""
101	    for port in range(start, end + 1):
102	        try:
103	            with socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as s:
104	                s.bind((host, port))
105	                return port
106	        except OSError:
107	            continue
108	    return None
109	
110	
111	def _detect_folder_type(folder: Path) -> str:
112	    """Detect whether folder contains jobs or tasks.
113	
114	    Returns 'jobs' or 'tasks'.
115	    """
116	    subdirs = sorted(
117	        (d for d in folder.iterdir() if d.is_dir() and not d.name.startswith(".")),
118	        key=lambda d: d.name,
119	    )
120	    if not subdirs:
121	        console.print(
122	            "[red]Error:[/red] Folder has no subdirectories. "
123	            "Use --tasks or --jobs to specify the folder type."
124	        )
125	        raise SystemExit(1)
126	
127	    for subdir in subdirs:
128	        if (subdir / "config.json").exists():
129	            return "jobs"
130	        if (subdir / "task.toml").exists():
131	            return "tasks"
132	
133	    console.print(
134	        "[red]Error:[/red] Could not auto-detect folder type. "
135	        "Use --tasks or --jobs to specify."
136	    )
137	    raise SystemExit(1)
138	
139	
140	def view_command(
141	    folder: Annotated[
142	        Path,
143	        Argument(
144	            help="Folder containing job directories or task definitions",
145	        ),
146	    ],
147	    port: Annotated[
148	        str,
149	        Option(
150	            "--port",
151	            "-p",
152	            help="Port or port range (e.g., 8080 or 8080-8090)",
153	        ),
154	    ] = "8080-8089",
155	    host: Annotated[
156	        str,
157	        Option(
158	            "--host",
159	            help="Host to bind the server to",
160	        ),
161	    ] = "127.0.0.1",
162	    dev: Annotated[
163	        bool,
164	        Option(
165	            "--dev",
166	            help="Run frontend in development mode with hot reloading",
167	        ),
168	    ] = False,
169	    no_build: Annotated[
170	        bool,
171	        Option(
172	            "--no-build",
173	            help="Skip auto-building viewer if static files are missing",
174	        ),
175	    ] = False,
176	    build: Annotated[
177	        bool,
178	        Option(
179	            "--build",
180	            help="Force rebuild of the viewer even if static files exist",
181	        ),
182	    ] = False,
183	    tasks: Annotated[
184	        bool,
185	        Option(
186	            "--tasks",
187	            help="Force task definitions mode",
188	        ),
189	    ] = False,
190	    jobs: Annotated[
191	        bool,
192	        Option(
193	            "--jobs",
194	            help="Force jobs mode",
195	        ),
196	    ] = False,
197	) -> None:
198	    """Start a web server to browse jobs or task definitions.
199	
200	    Auto-detects whether the folder contains jobs or task definitions.
201	    Use --tasks or --jobs to override detection.
202	
203	    Example usage:
204	        harbor view ./my-tasks          # auto-detects tasks
205	        harbor view ./my-jobs            # auto-detects jobs
206	        harbor view ./tasks --tasks      # force tasks mode
207	        harbor view ./jobs --port 9000
208	        harbor view ./jobs --dev
209	    """
210	    folder = folder.expanduser().resolve()
211	    if not folder.exists():
212	        console.print(f"[red]Error:[/red] Folder '{folder}' does not exist")
213	        raise SystemExit(1)
214	
215	    if tasks and jobs:
216	        console.print("[red]Error:[/red] Cannot specify both --tasks and --jobs")
217	        raise SystemExit(1)
218	
219	    # Determine mode
220	    if tasks:
221	        mode = "tasks"
222	    elif jobs:
223	        mode = "jobs"
224	    else:
225	        mode = _detect_folder_type(folder)
226	
227	    start_port, end_port = _parse_port_range(port)
228	    backend_port = _find_available_port(host, start_port, end_port)
229	
230	    if backend_port is None:
231	        console.print(
232	            f"[red]Error:[/red] No available port found in range {start_port}-{end_port}"
233	        )
234	        raise SystemExit(1)
235	
236	    if dev:
237	        if build:
238	            console.print(
239	                "[yellow]Warning:[/yellow] --build is ignored in dev mode "
240	                "(dev mode uses hot reloading instead of static files)"
241	            )
242	            console.print()
243	        _run_dev_mode(folder, host, backend_port, mode=mode)
244	    else:
245	        _run_production_mode(
246	            folder, host, backend_port, mode=mode, no_build=no_build, build=build
247	        )
248	
249	
250	def _run_production_mode(
251	    folder: Path,
252	    host: str,
253	    port: int,
254	    *,
255	    mode: str = "jobs",
256	    no_build: bool = False,
257	    build: bool = False,
258	) -> None:
259	    """Run in production mode with static files served from the package."""
260	    import uvicorn
261	
262	    from harbor.viewer import create_app
263	
264	    static_dir = STATIC_DIR if STATIC_DIR.exists() else None
265	
266	    # Force build if requested
267	    if build:
268	        if not VIEWER_DIR.exists():
269	            console.print(
270	                f"[red]Error:[/red] Cannot build: viewer source not found at {VIEWER_DIR}"
271	            )
272	            raise SystemExit(1)
273	        console.print("[blue]Force rebuilding viewer...[/blue]")
274	        console.print()
275	        if _build_viewer():
276	            static_dir = STATIC_DIR
277	        else:
278	            console.print()
279	            console.print("[red]Build failed.[/red]")
280	            raise SystemExit(1)
281	    # Auto-build if static files are missing and we have the source
282	    elif static_dir is None and not no_build and VIEWER_DIR.exists():
283	        console.print(
284	            "[yellow]Viewer not built.[/yellow] Building now (this only happens once)..."
285	        )
286	        console.print()
287	        if _build_viewer():
288	            static_dir = STATIC_DIR
289	        else:
290	            console.print()
291	            console.print("[yellow]Build failed.[/yellow] Starting in API-only mode.")
292	            console.print("  You can also try --dev flag for development mode.")
293	            console.print()
294	
295	    if static_dir is None:
296	        console.print(
297	            "[yellow]Warning:[/yellow] Viewer static files not found. "
298	            "Only API will be available."
299	        )
300	        console.print("  Use --dev flag for development mode with hot reloading.")
301	        console.print()
302	
303	    app = create_app(folder, mode=mode, static_dir=static_dir)
304	
305	    folder_label = "Tasks folder" if mode == "tasks" else "Jobs folder"
306	    console.print("[green]Starting Harbor Viewer[/green]")
307	    console.print(f"  {folder_label}: {folder}")
308	    console.print(f"  Mode: {mode}")
309	    console.print(f"  Server: http://{host}:{port}")
310	    if static_dir is None:
311	        console.print(f"  API docs: http://{host}:{port}/docs")
312	    console.print()
313	
314	    config = uvicorn.Config(app, host=host, port=port, log_level="info")
315	    server = uvicorn.Server(config)
316	    server.run()
317	
318	
319	def _run_dev_mode(
320	    folder: Path, host: str, backend_port: int, *, mode: str = "jobs"
321	) -> None:
322	    """Run in development mode with separate backend and frontend dev server."""
323	    import uvicorn
324	
325	    if not VIEWER_DIR.exists():
326	        console.print(f"[red]Error:[/red] Viewer directory not found at {VIEWER_DIR}")
327	        console.print("  Dev mode requires the viewer source code.")
328	        raise SystemExit(1)
329	
330	    if not _has_bun():
331	        console.print(
332	            "[red]Error:[/red] bun is required for dev mode. "
333	            "Install it from https://bun.com"
334	        )
335	        raise SystemExit(1)
336	
337	    folder_label = "Tasks folder" if mode == "tasks" else "Jobs folder"
338	    console.print("[green]Starting Harbor Viewer (dev mode)[/green]")
339	    console.print(f"  {folder_label}: {folder}")
340	    console.print(f"  Mode: {mode}")
341	    console.print(f"  Backend API: http://{host}:{backend_port}")
342	    console.print("  Frontend: http://localhost:5173")
343	    console.print()
344	
345	    # Install frontend dependencies
346	    console.print("  Installing frontend dependencies...")
347	    result = subprocess.run(
348	        ["bun", "install"],
349	        cwd=VIEWER_DIR,
350	        capture_output=True,
351	        text=True,
352	    )
353	    if result.returncode != 0:
354	        console.print(f"[red]Error:[/red] bun install failed:\n{result.stderr}")
355	        raise SystemExit(1)
356	
357	    # Start frontend dev server in subprocess
358	    frontend_env = os.environ.copy()
359	    frontend_env["VITE_API_URL"] = f"http://{host}:{backend_port}"
360	
361	    frontend_proc = subprocess.Popen(
362	        ["bun", "run", "dev"],
363	        cwd=VIEWER_DIR,
364	        env=frontend_env,
365	    )
366	
367	    def cleanup_frontend() -> None:
368	        frontend_proc.terminate()
369	        try:
370	            frontend_proc.wait(timeout=5)
371	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
372	            frontend_proc.kill()
373	
374	    # Set environment variables for the factory function to use
375	    os.environ["HARBOR_VIEWER_FOLDER"] = str(folder)
376	    os.environ["HARBOR_VIEWER_MODE"] = mode
377	
378	    # Run backend - uvicorn handles SIGINT/SIGTERM and shuts down gracefully
379	    try:
380	        uvicorn.run(
381	            "harbor.viewer:create_app_from_env",
382	            host=host,
383	            port=backend_port,
384	            log_level="info",
385	            reload=True,
386	            reload_dirs=[str(Path(__file__).parent.parent / "viewer")],
387	        )
388	    finally:
389	        # Always clean up frontend after uvicorn exits
390	        cleanup_frontend()
391

file.content:
"""CLI command for trajectory web viewer."""

import os
import socket
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Annotated

from rich.console import Console
from typer import Argument, Option

console = Console(stderr=True)

# Path to static viewer files (built in CI)
STATIC_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "viewer" / "static"

# Path to viewer source (for dev mode)
VIEWER_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent.parent / "apps" / "viewer"


def _parse_port_range(port_str: str) -> tuple[int, int]:
    """Parse a port or port range string into (start, end) tuple."""
    if "-" in port_str:
        parts = port_str.split("-")
        return int(parts[0]), int(parts[1])
    port = int(port_str)
    return port, port


def _has_bun() -> bool:
    """Check if bun is available."""
    import shutil

    return shutil.which("bun") is not None


def _build_viewer() -> bool:
    """Build the viewer frontend and copy to static directory.

    Returns True on success, False on failure.
    """
    if not VIEWER_DIR.exists():
        console.print(f"[red]Error:[/red] Viewer source not found at {VIEWER_DIR}")
        return False

    if not _has_bun():
        console.print(
            "[red]Error:[/red] bun is required to build the viewer. "
            "Install it from https://bun.com"
        )
        return False

    console.print("[blue]Building viewer...[/blue]")

    # Install dependencies
    console.print("  Installing dependencies...")
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["bun", "install"],
        cwd=VIEWER_DIR,
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
    )
    if result.returncode != 0:
        console.print("[red]Error:[/red] Failed to install dependencies")
        console.print(result.stderr)
        return False

    # Build
    console.print("  Building frontend...")
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["bun", "run", "build"],
        cwd=VIEWER_DIR,
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
    )
    if result.returncode != 0:
        console.print("[red]Error:[/red] Build failed")
        console.print(result.stderr)
        return False

    # Copy build output to static directory
    build_client_dir = VIEWER_DIR / "build" / "client"
    if not build_client_dir.exists():
        console.print(f"[red]Error:[/red] Build output not found at {build_client_dir}")
        return False

    import shutil

    # Remove existing static dir if present
    if STATIC_DIR.exists():
        shutil.rmtree(STATIC_DIR)

    # Copy build output
    shutil.copytree(build_client_dir, STATIC_DIR)
    console.print("[green]Build complete![/green]")
    return True


def _find_available_port(host: str, start: int, end: int) -> int | None:
    """Find an available port in the given range."""
    for port in range(start, end + 1):
        try:
            with socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as s:
                s.bind((host, port))
                return port
        except OSError:
            continue
    return None


def _detect_folder_type(folder: Path) -> str:
    """Detect whether folder contains jobs or tasks.

    Returns 'jobs' or 'tasks'.
    """
    subdirs = sorted(
        (d for d in folder.iterdir() if d.is_dir() and not d.name.startswith(".")),
        key=lambda d: d.name,
    )
    if not subdirs:
        console.print(
            "[red]Error:[/red] Folder has no subdirectories. "
            "Use --tasks or --jobs to specify the folder type."
        )
        raise SystemExit(1)

    for subdir in subdirs:
        if (subdir / "config.json").exists():
            return "jobs"
        if (subdir / "task.toml").exists():
            return "tasks"

    console.print(
        "[red]Error:[/red] Could not auto-detect folder type. "
        "Use --tasks or --jobs to specify."
    )
    raise SystemExit(1)


def view_command(
    folder: Annotated[
        Path,
        Argument(
            help="Folder containing job directories or task definitions",
        ),
    ],
    port: Annotated[
        str,
        Option(
            "--port",
            "-p",
            help="Port or port range (e.g., 8080 or 8080-8090)",
        ),
    ] = "8080-8089",
    host: Annotated[
        str,
        Option(
            "--host",
            help="Host to bind the server to",
        ),
    ] = "127.0.0.1",
    dev: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--dev",
            help="Run frontend in development mode with hot reloading",
        ),
    ] = False,
    no_build: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--no-build",
            help="Skip auto-building viewer if static files are missing",
        ),
    ] = False,
    build: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--build",
            help="Force rebuild of the viewer even if static files exist",
        ),
    ] = False,
    tasks: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--tasks",
            help="Force task definitions mode",
        ),
    ] = False,
    jobs: Annotated[
        bool,
        Option(
            "--jobs",
            help="Force jobs mode",
        ),
    ] = False,
) -> None:
    """Start a web server to browse jobs or task definitions.

    Auto-detects whether the folder contains jobs or task definitions.
    Use --tasks or --jobs to override detection.

    Example usage:
        harbor view ./my-tasks          # auto-detects tasks
        harbor view ./my-jobs            # auto-detects jobs
        harbor view ./tasks --tasks      # force tasks mode
        harbor view ./jobs --port 9000
        harbor view ./jobs --dev
    """
    folder = folder.expanduser().resolve()
    if not folder.exists():
        console.print(f"[red]Error:[/red] Folder '{folder}' does not exist")
        raise SystemExit(1)

    if tasks and jobs:
        console.print("[red]Error:[/red] Cannot specify both --tasks and --jobs")
        raise SystemExit(1)

    # Determine mode
    if tasks:
        mode = "tasks"
    elif jobs:
        mode = "jobs"
    else:
        mode = _detect_folder_type(folder)

    start_port, end_port = _parse_port_range(port)
    backend_port = _find_available_port(host, start_port, end_port)

    if backend_port is None:
        console.print(
            f"[red]Error:[/red] No available port found in range {start_port}-{end_port}"
        )
        raise SystemExit(1)

    if dev:
        if build:
            console.print(
                "[yellow]Warning:[/yellow] --build is ignored in dev mode "
                "(dev mode uses hot reloading instead of static files)"
            )
            console.print()
        _run_dev_mode(folder, host, backend_port, mode=mode)
    else:
        _run_production_mode(
            folder, host, backend_port, mode=mode, no_build=no_build, build=build
        )


def _run_production_mode(
    folder: Path,
    host: str,
    port: int,
    *,
    mode: str = "jobs",
    no_build: bool = False,
    build: bool = False,
) -> None:
    """Run in production mode with static files served from the package."""
    import uvicorn

    from harbor.viewer import create_app

    static_dir = STATIC_DIR if STATIC_DIR.exists() else None

    # Force build if requested
    if build:
        if not VIEWER_DIR.exists():
            console.print(
                f"[red]Error:[/red] Cannot build: viewer source not found at {VIEWER_DIR}"
            )
            raise SystemExit(1)
        console.print("[blue]Force rebuilding viewer...[/blue]")
        console.print()
        if _build_viewer():
            static_dir = STATIC_DIR
        else:
            console.print()
            console.print("[red]Build failed.[/red]")
            raise SystemExit(1)
    # Auto-build if static files are missing and we have the source
    elif static_dir is None and not no_build and VIEWER_DIR.exists():
        console.print(
            "[yellow]Viewer not built.[/yellow] Building now (this only happens once)..."
        )
        console.print()
        if _build_viewer():
            static_dir = STATIC_DIR
        else:
            console.print()
            console.print("[yellow]Build failed.[/yellow] Starting in API-only mode.")
            console.print("  You can also try --dev flag for development mode.")
            console.print()

    if static_dir is None:
        console.print(
            "[yellow]Warning:[/yellow] Viewer static files not found. "
            "Only API will be available."
        )
        console.print("  Use --dev flag for development mode with hot reloading.")
        console.print()

    app = create_app(folder, mode=mode, static_dir=static_dir)

    folder_label = "Tasks folder" if mode == "tasks" else "Jobs folder"
    console.print("[green]Starting Harbor Viewer[/green]")
    console.print(f"  {folder_label}: {folder}")
    console.print(f"  Mode: {mode}")
    console.print(f"  Server: http://{host}:{port}")
    if static_dir is None:
        console.print(f"  API docs: http://{host}:{port}/docs")
    console.print()

    config = uvicorn.Config(app, host=host, port=port, log_level="info")
    server = uvicorn.Server(config)
    server.run()


def _run_dev_mode(
    folder: Path, host: str, backend_port: int, *, mode: str = "jobs"
) -> None:
    """Run in development mode with separate backend and frontend dev server."""
    import uvicorn

    if not VIEWER_DIR.exists():
        console.print(f"[red]Error:[/red] Viewer directory not found at {VIEWER_DIR}")
        console.print("  Dev mode requires the viewer source code.")
        raise SystemExit(1)

    if not _has_bun():
        console.print(
            "[red]Error:[/red] bun is required for dev mode. "
            "Install it from https://bun.com"
        )
        raise SystemExit(1)

    folder_label = "Tasks folder" if mode == "tasks" else "Jobs folder"
    console.print("[green]Starting Harbor Viewer (dev mode)[/green]")
    console.print(f"  {folder_label}: {folder}")
    console.print(f"  Mode: {mode}")
    console.print(f"  Backend API: http://{host}:{backend_port}")
    console.print("  Frontend: http://localhost:5173")
    console.print()

    # Install frontend dependencies
    console.print("  Installing frontend dependencies...")
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["bun", "install"],
        cwd=VIEWER_DIR,
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
    )
    if result.returncode != 0:
        console.print(f"[red]Error:[/red] bun install failed:\n{result.stderr}")
        raise SystemExit(1)

    # Start frontend dev server in subprocess
    frontend_env = os.environ.copy()
    frontend_env["VITE_API_URL"] = f"http://{host}:{backend_port}"

    frontend_proc = subprocess.Popen(
        ["bun", "run", "dev"],
        cwd=VIEWER_DIR,
        env=frontend_env,
    )

    def cleanup_frontend() -> None:
        frontend_proc.terminate()
        try:
            frontend_proc.wait(timeout=5)
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            frontend_proc.kill()

    # Set environment variables for the factory function to use
    os.environ["HARBOR_VIEWER_FOLDER"] = str(folder)
    os.environ["HARBOR_VIEWER_MODE"] = mode

    # Run backend - uvicorn handles SIGINT/SIGTERM and shuts down gracefully
    try:
        uvicorn.run(
            "harbor.viewer:create_app_from_env",
            host=host,
            port=backend_port,
            log_level="info",
            reload=True,
            reload_dirs=[str(Path(__file__).parent.parent / "viewer")],
        )
    finally:
        # Always clean up frontend after uvicorn exits
        cleanup_frontend()

content.subject: Research core architecture & execution lifecycle
content.description:
Investigate Harbor's core abstractions and how a run flows end-to-end. Focus on:
- `src/harbor/agents/base.py` (BaseAgent abstract class), `agents/factory.py`
- `src/harbor/environments/base.py` (BaseEnvironment), `environments/factory.py`
- `src/harbor/orchestrators/` — how trials are orchestrated (concurrency, retries)
- `src/harbor/trial/` and `src/harbor/job.py` — trial vs job models and lifecycle
- `src/harbor/models/` — Pydantic v2 data models (agent, job, task, trial, metric, verifier)
- The async execution lifecycle: environment.start → agent.setup → agent.run → verifier.verify → environment.stop
- `src/harbor/__init__.py` lazy-import pattern

Deliver: a structured summary (key files with paths, the main classes/interfaces, the end-to-end flow of `harbor run`, concurrency model, and notable design patterns). Cite file paths. Report findings back to the team lead via SendMessage when done, then mark this task completed.

content.subject: Research agents & environment backends
content.description:
Survey Harbor's agent implementations and execution environment backends. Focus on:
- `src/harbor/agents/installed/` — the 18+ built-in agents (claude-code, openhands, codex, aider, goose, gemini-cli, etc.): how they subclass BaseAgent, setup/run patterns, env var handling, `--ae`/`--agent-env`
- `src/harbor/agents/installed/base.py` (BaseInstalledAgent) if present, `factory.py`, registration in `models/agent/name.py` (AgentName enum)
- `src/harbor/agents/terminus_2/` and internal Terminus agents
- `src/harbor/environments/` backends: docker, daytona, e2b, modal, runloop, apple_container, gke, openshift, novita — what each provides, how BaseEnvironment is implemented (start/stop/exec/upload), EnvironmentType enum & factory
- SUPPORTS_ATIF and SUPPORTS_WINDOWS flags

Deliver: a structured summary (catalog of agents with one-line each, catalog of environments, how to add a new agent/environment, key shared patterns). Cite file paths. Report findings back to the team lead via SendMessage when done, then mark this task completed.

content.subject: Research the CLI & command surface
content.description:
Survey Harbor's command-line interface and user-facing workflows. Focus on:
- `src/harbor/cli/main.py` — entry point, how command groups are registered (app.add_typer), the `harbor`/`hr`/`hb` scripts
- Each command module in `src/harbor/cli/`: run/jobs/datasets/trials/tasks/traces/sweeps/adapters/adapter_wizard/publish/analyze/cache/view, plus `admin/`, `annotator/`, `debug_checker/`, `quality_checker/`, `template-*`
- The primary `harbor run` command — its flags (--dataset, --agent, --model, --n-concurrent, --ae, --agent-import-path combined into --agent per recent commit), how it maps to jobs/trials
- Sweeps (parameter sweeps), traces viewing, analysis backend
- Recently added flags: agent/verifier log-filter flags on `harbor trial start`

Deliver: a structured summary (the command tree, the main user workflows from CLI, key flags, how the CLI maps onto the core models). Cite file paths. Report findings back to the team lead via SendMessage when done, then mark this task completed.

content.subject: Research benchmarks, verifier, metrics & registry
content.description:
Survey Harbor's benchmark adapter system, verification, metrics, and dataset registry. Focus on:
- `adapters/` — how the 50+ adapters convert external benchmarks (SWE-Bench, Terminal-Bench, Aider Polyglot, LiveCodeBench, etc.) to Harbor task format; the adapter.py/run_adapter.py/template structure
- The task format: task.toml, instruction.md, environment/, tests/ (test.sh writes reward to /logs/verifier/reward.txt or reward.json), solution/
- `src/harbor/verifier/` — the test verification system; `src/harbor/models/verifier/`
- `src/harbor/metrics/` — metrics collection incl. pass@k (recently added unit tests, commit 496293b0)
- `src/harbor/registry/` and `registry.json` (13.9MB!) + `src/harbor/dataset/` — dataset registry, how datasets are listed/resolved (`harbor datasets list`, `dataset@version` syntax)
- `src/harbor/models/trajectories/` — the ATIF (Agent Trajectory Interchange Format)
- `src/harbor/leaderboard/`

Deliver: a structured summary (how adapters work, the canonical task format, the verifier contract, metrics incl pass@k, how the registry/datasets work, ATIF overview). Cite file paths. Report findings back to the team lead via SendMessage when done, then mark this task completed.

content.subject: Research web apps, storage, packages & CI/CD
content.description:
Survey Harbor's web apps, storage, publishing, packages, and CI/CD. Focus on:
- `apps/viewer/` — the results viewer web app (React Router + Vite): what it visualizes, tech stack, how it gets data
- `docs/` — the documentation site (Next.js + Fumadocs)
- `src/harbor/storage/` (Supabase backends), `src/harbor/db/`, `src/harbor/auth/` (OAuth callback server) — note the Supabase/PostgREST pagination rules from AGENTS.md
- `src/harbor/publisher/` — package publishing & registry DB; `src/harbor/upload/` and `src/harbor/download/`
- `packages/` — the uv workspace members (harbor-rewardkit, harbor-langsmith); how `pyproject.toml [tool.uv.workspace]` ties them
- `.github/workflows/` — CI/CD (pytest, ruff, ty, claude-code-review, sync-registry, adapter-review, etc.)
- `examples/training/` and the RL optimization claim — how Harbor generates rollouts for RL
- `rfcs/` and `skills/`

Deliver: a structured summary (viewer app overview, docs, storage/auth model, publisher/registry, workspace packages, CI matrix, RL/training hooks). Cite file paths. Report findings back to the team lead via SendMessage when done, then mark this task completed.

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

import typer
from rich.console import Console
from rich.table import Table

from harbor.cli.utils import run_async
from harbor.models.environment_type import EnvironmentType

console = Console()

_OUTCOME_STYLES = {
    "pass": "green",
    "fail": "red",
    "not_applicable": "grey50",
}


def _outcome_str(check) -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Extract (outcome, explanation) strings from a check, handling enums and dicts."""
    if isinstance(check, dict):
        return str(check.get("outcome", "")), str(check.get("explanation", ""))
    outcome = check.outcome
    outcome_s = outcome.value if hasattr(outcome, "value") else str(outcome)
    return outcome_s, str(check.explanation)


def _render_checks_table(
    title: str, checks: dict[str, Any], summary: str | None = None
):
    """Render a Rich table for rubric check results."""
    table = Table(title=title, show_lines=True)
    table.add_column("Check")
    table.add_column("Outcome")
    table.add_column("Explanation")

    for name, check in checks.items():
        outcome, explanation = _outcome_str(check)
        table.add_row(
            name.replace("_", " ").title(),
            outcome,
            explanation,
            style=_OUTCOME_STYLES.get(outcome, "white"),
        )

    if summary:
        console.print(f"\n[bold]Summary:[/bold] {summary}\n")
    console.print(table)


def _render_check_summary(report) -> None:
    """Render a one-row-per-task summary table for a multi-task check."""
    table = Table(title="Task Quality Checks", show_lines=True)
    for col in ("Task", "Pass", "Fail", "N/A", "Cost ($)"):
        table.add_column(col)

    for r in report.results:
        if r.error:
            table.add_row(r.task_name or "?", "-", "-", "-", "-", style="red")
            continue
        counts = {"pass": 0, "fail": 0, "not_applicable": 0}
        for check in r.checks.values():
            outcome, _ = _outcome_str(check)
            counts[outcome] = counts.get(outcome, 0) + 1
        table.add_row(
            r.task_name or "?",
            str(counts["pass"]),
            str(counts["fail"]),
            str(counts["not_applicable"]),
            f"{r.cost_usd:.4f}" if r.cost_usd is not None else "-",
            style="red" if counts["fail"] else "white",
        )

    console.print(table)
    for r in report.results:
        if r.error:
            console.print(f"[red]❌ {r.task_name}: {r.error.splitlines()[0]}[/red]")
    total = report.total_cost_usd
    if total is not None:
        console.print(f"[dim]Total agent cost: ${total:.4f}[/dim]")


def check_command(
    path: Path = typer.Argument(
        ..., help="Path to a task directory or a directory of task directories"
    ),
    rubric: Path | None = typer.Option(
        None,
        "-r",
        "--rubric",
        help="Rubric file (TOML/YAML/JSON). Uses built-in default if not specified.",
    ),
    prompt: Path | None = typer.Option(
        None,
        "-p",
        "--prompt",
        help="Prompt file for the evaluator agent. Uses built-in default if not specified.",
    ),
    agent: str = typer.Option("claude-code", "-a", "--agent", help="Agent to use"),
    model: str = typer.Option(
        "claude-sonnet-4-6", "-m", "--model", help="Model to use"
    ),
    agent_kwargs: list[str] | None = typer.Option(
        None, "--ak", "--agent-kwarg", help="Agent kwarg key=value (repeatable)"
    ),
    agent_env: list[str] | None = typer.Option(
        None, "--ae", "--agent-env", help="Env var KEY=VALUE for the agent (repeatable)"
    ),
    environment: EnvironmentType = typer.Option(
        EnvironmentType.DOCKER,
        "-e",
        "--env",
        help="Environment type to run the check in (e.g. docker, daytona).",
    ),
    environment_kwargs: list[str] | None = typer.Option(
        None,
        "--ek",
        "--environment-kwarg",
        help="Environment kwarg key=value (repeatable)",
    ),
    n_concurrent: int = typer.Option(
        4, "-n", "--n-concurrent", help="Max concurrent task checks"
    ),
    n_attempts: int = typer.Option(1, "-k", "--n-attempts", help="Attempts per task"),
    include_task_names: list[str] | None = typer.Option(
        None,
        "-i",
        "--include-task-name",
        help="Only check tasks matching glob (repeatable)",
    ),
    exclude_task_names: list[str] | None = typer.Option(
        None, "-x", "--exclude-task-name", help="Skip tasks matching glob (repeatable)"
    ),
    n_tasks: int | None = typer.Option(
        None, "-l", "--n-tasks", help="Max tasks to check"
    ),
    job_name: str | None = typer.Option(
        None, "--job-name", help="Job name (default: timestamp)"
    ),
    jobs_dir: Path | None = typer.Option(
        None, "-o", "--jobs-dir", help="Directory to store job results (default: jobs)"
    ),
    config: Path | None = typer.Option(
        None, "-c", "--config", help="Base JobConfig (YAML/JSON) for advanced settings"
    ),
    quiet: bool = typer.Option(False, "-q", "--quiet", help="Suppress trial progress"),
):
    """Check task quality against a rubric.

    PATH may be a single task directory or a directory of task directories; each
    task is checked as a trial in one Harbor job.
    """
    from harbor.analyze.checker import run_checks
    from harbor.cli.utils import parse_env_vars, parse_kwargs

    console.print("\n[blue]🔎 Checking task quality...[/blue]")

    try:
        report, job_dir = run_async(
            run_checks(
                path=path,
                agent=agent,
                model=model,
                rubric_path=rubric,
                prompt_path=prompt,
                environment=environment,
                n_concurrent=n_concurrent,
                n_attempts=n_attempts,
                job_name=job_name,
                jobs_dir=jobs_dir,
                agent_kwargs=parse_kwargs(agent_kwargs),
                agent_env=parse_env_vars(agent_env),
                environment_kwargs=parse_kwargs(environment_kwargs),
                include_task_names=include_task_names,
                exclude_task_names=exclude_task_names,
                n_tasks=n_tasks,
                config_path=config,
                quiet=quiet,
            )
        )
    except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError, RuntimeError) as e:
        console.print(f"[red]❌ {e}[/red]")
        raise typer.Exit(1)

    if len(report.results) == 1:
        result = report.results[0]
        if result.error:
            console.print(f"[red]❌ {result.task_name}: {result.error}[/red]")
        else:
            _render_checks_table(
                f"Task Quality Checks: {result.task_name}", result.checks
            )
            if result.cost_usd is not None:
                console.print(f"[dim]Agent cost: ${result.cost_usd:.4f}[/dim]")
    else:
        _render_check_summary(report)

    console.print(f"\n[bold]Report:[/bold] {job_dir / 'check_report.json'}")
    console.print(f"Inspect results by running `harbor view {job_dir.parent}`")
    console.print(
        "[dim]In the viewer, open a trial's Artifacts tab to read its "
        "check-result.json, and use ← / → to switch between tasks.[/dim]"
    )

    if any(r.error for r in report.results):
        raise typer.Exit(1)


def _render_analyze_summary(report) -> None:
    """Render a one-row-per-trial summary table for a multi-trial analysis."""
    table = Table(title="Trial Analyses", show_lines=True)
    for col in ("Trial", "Pass", "Fail", "N/A", "Cost ($)"):
        table.add_column(col)

    for r in report.results:
        if r.error:
            table.add_row(r.trial_name or "?", "-", "-", "-", "-", style="red")
            continue
        counts = {"pass": 0, "fail": 0, "not_applicable": 0}
        for check in r.checks.values():
            outcome, _ = _outcome_str(check)
            counts[outcome] = counts.get(outcome, 0) + 1
        table.add_row(
            r.trial_name or "?",
            str(counts["pass"]),
            str(counts["fail"]),
            str(counts["not_applicable"]),
            f"{r.cost_usd:.4f}" if r.cost_usd is not None else "-",
            style="red" if counts["fail"] else "white",
        )

    console.print(table)
    for r in report.results:
        if r.error:
            console.print(f"[red]❌ {r.trial_name}: {r.error.splitlines()[0]}[/red]")
    total = report.total_cost_usd
    if total is not None:
        console.print(f"[dim]Total agent cost: ${total:.4f}[/dim]")


def analyze_command(
    path: Path = typer.Argument(
        ..., help="Path to a trial directory or a job directory of trials"
    ),
    rubric: Path | None = typer.Option(
        None,
        "-r",
        "--rubric",
        help="Rubric file (TOML/YAML/JSON). Uses built-in default (reward_hacking, task_specification) if not specified.",
    ),
    prompt: Path | None = typer.Option(
        None,
        "-p",
        "--prompt",
        help="Prompt file for the evaluator agent. Uses built-in default if not specified.",
    ),
    agent: str = typer.Option("claude-code", "-a", "--agent", help="Agent to use"),
    model: str = typer.Option("claude-haiku-4-5", "-m", "--model", help="Model to use"),
    agent_kwargs: list[str] | None = typer.Option(
        None, "--ak", "--agent-kwarg", help="Agent kwarg key=value (repeatable)"
    ),
    agent_env: list[str] | None = typer.Option(
        None, "--ae", "--agent-env", help="Env var KEY=VALUE for the agent (repeatable)"
    ),
    environment: EnvironmentType = typer.Option(
        EnvironmentType.DOCKER,
        "-e",
        "--env",
        help="Environment type to run the analysis in (e.g. docker, daytona).",
    ),
    environment_kwargs: list[str] | None = typer.Option(
        None,
        "--ek",
        "--environment-kwarg",
        help="Environment kwarg key=value (repeatable)",
    ),
    n_concurrent: int = typer.Option(
        4, "-n", "--n-concurrent", help="Max concurrent trial analyses"
    ),
    n_attempts: int = typer.Option(1, "-k", "--n-attempts", help="Attempts per trial"),
    passing: bool = typer.Option(
        False, "--passing", help="Only analyze passing trials (reward=1.0)"
    ),
    failing: bool = typer.Option(
        False, "--failing", help="Only analyze failing trials (reward<1.0 or exception)"
    ),
    n_trials: int | None = typer.Option(
        None, "-l", "--n-trials", help="Max trials to analyze"
    ),
    job_name: str | None = typer.Option(
        None, "--job-name", help="Job name (default: timestamp)"
    ),
    jobs_dir: Path | None = typer.Option(
        None, "-o", "--jobs-dir", help="Directory to store job results (default: jobs)"
    ),
    config: Path | None = typer.Option(
        None, "-c", "--config", help="Base JobConfig (YAML/JSON) for advanced settings"
    ),
    quiet: bool = typer.Option(False, "-q", "--quiet", help="Suppress trial progress"),
):
    """Analyze trial trajectories against a rubric.

    PATH may be a single trial directory or a job directory of trials; each trial
    is analyzed as a trial in one Harbor job.
    """
    from harbor.analyze.analyzer import run_analyze
    from harbor.cli.utils import parse_env_vars, parse_kwargs

    if passing and failing:
        console.print("[red]❌ Cannot use both --passing and --failing[/red]")
        raise typer.Exit(1)

    filter_passing: bool | None = True if passing else (False if failing else None)

    console.print("\n[blue]🔍 Analyzing trial(s)...[/blue]")

    try:
        report, job_dir = run_async(
            run_analyze(
                path=path,
                agent=agent,
                model=model,
                rubric_path=rubric,
                prompt_path=prompt,
                environment=environment,
                n_concurrent=n_concurrent,
                n_attempts=n_attempts,
                filter_passing=filter_passing,
                job_name=job_name,
                jobs_dir=jobs_dir,
                agent_kwargs=parse_kwargs(agent_kwargs),
                agent_env=parse_env_vars(agent_env),
                environment_kwargs=parse_kwargs(environment_kwargs),
                n_trials=n_trials,
                config_path=config,
                quiet=quiet,
            )
        )
    except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError, RuntimeError) as e:
        console.print(f"[red]❌ {e}[/red]")
        raise typer.Exit(1)

    if len(report.results) == 1:
        result = report.results[0]
        if result.error:
            console.print(f"[red]❌ {result.trial_name}: {result.error}[/red]")
        else:
            _render_checks_table(
                f"Trial: {result.trial_name}", result.checks, summary=result.summary
            )
            if result.cost_usd is not None:
                console.print(f"[dim]Agent cost: ${result.cost_usd:.4f}[/dim]")
    else:
        _render_analyze_summary(report)

    console.print(f"\n[bold]Report:[/bold] {job_dir / 'analysis.json'}")
    console.print(
        f"[dim]Inspect with `harbor view {job_dir.parent}` — two ways to read "
        "the analysis:[/dim]"
    )
    console.print("[dim]  1. Open the analyzed job → a trial's Analysis tab.[/dim]")
    console.print(
        "[dim]  2. Open this job → a trial's Artifacts tab (analysis.json).[/dim]"
    )

    if any(r.error for r in report.results):
        raise typer.Exit(1)

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from pathlib import Path
4	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Annotated
5	
6	from typer import Argument, Option, echo
7	
8	if TYPE_CHECKING:
9	    from rich.console import Console
10	
11	    from harbor.publisher.publisher import Publisher
12	
13	
14	def _humanize_bytes(n: int) -> str:
15	    for unit in ("B", "KB", "MB", "GB"):
16	        if n < 1024:
17	            return f"{n:.1f} {unit}" if unit != "B" else f"{n} {unit}"
18	        n /= 1024  # ty: ignore[invalid-assignment]
19	    return f"{n:.1f} TB"
20	
21	
22	def _format_exception(exc: BaseException) -> str:
23	    from postgrest.exceptions import APIError
24	
25	    message = (
26	        (exc.message or str(exc)).strip()
27	        if isinstance(exc, APIError)
28	        else str(exc).strip()
29	    )
30	    detail = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {message}" if message else type(exc).__name__
31	    notes = getattr(exc, "__notes__", None) or []
32	    return f"{detail} ({'; '.join(notes)})" if notes else detail
33	
34	
35	def _resolve_paths(
36	    paths: list[Path], *, no_tasks: bool = False
37	) -> tuple[list[Path], list[Path], set[Path], set[Path]]:
38	    """Resolve paths to task and dataset directories by auto-detecting type.
39	
40	    Returns (task_dirs, dataset_dirs, explicit_task_dirs, explicit_dataset_dirs).
41	    ``explicit_task_dirs`` contains only those task dirs that the user directly
42	    specified (not auto-discovered from dataset or parent directories).
43	    ``explicit_dataset_dirs`` contains only those dataset dirs that the user
44	    directly specified.
45	    """
46	    from harbor.models.dataset.paths import DatasetPaths
47	    from harbor.models.task.paths import TaskPaths
48	
49	    task_dirs: list[Path] = []
50	    dataset_dirs: list[Path] = []
51	    explicit_task_dirs: set[Path] = set()
52	    explicit_dataset_dirs: set[Path] = set()
53	
54	    for p in paths:
55	        resolved = p.resolve()
56	        # dataset.toml file passed directly
57	        if resolved.is_file() and resolved.name == DatasetPaths.MANIFEST_FILENAME:
58	            dataset_dirs.append(resolved.parent)
59	            explicit_dataset_dirs.add(resolved.parent)
60	            continue
61	        if not resolved.is_dir():
62	            echo(f"Warning: {p} is not a directory, skipping.")
63	            continue
64	        has_task = (resolved / TaskPaths.CONFIG_FILENAME).exists()
65	        has_dataset = (resolved / DatasetPaths.MANIFEST_FILENAME).exists()
66	        if has_task and has_dataset:
67	            echo(
68	                f"Error: {p} contains both {TaskPaths.CONFIG_FILENAME} and "
69	                f"{DatasetPaths.MANIFEST_FILENAME}. Cannot determine type."
70	            )
71	            raise SystemExit(1)
72	        if has_task:
73	            task_dirs.append(resolved)
74	            explicit_task_dirs.add(resolved)
75	        elif has_dataset:
76	            dataset_dirs.append(resolved)
77	            explicit_dataset_dirs.add(resolved)
78	            # Also collect task subdirs so they are published before the dataset
79	            if not no_tasks:
80	                for child in sorted(resolved.iterdir()):
81	                    if child.is_dir() and (child / TaskPaths.CONFIG_FILENAME).exists():
82	                        task_dirs.append(child)
83	        else:
84	            # Scan immediate subdirectories
85	            found_any = False
86	            for child in sorted(resolved.iterdir()):
87	                if not child.is_dir():
88	                    continue
89	                if (child / TaskPaths.CONFIG_FILENAME).exists():
90	                    task_dirs.append(child)
91	                    explicit_task_dirs.add(child)
92	                    found_any = True
93	            if not found_any:
94	                echo(f"Warning: {p} contains no tasks, skipping.")
95	
96	    return task_dirs, dataset_dirs, explicit_task_dirs, explicit_dataset_dirs
97	
98	
99	def publish_command(
100	    paths: Annotated[
101	        list[Path] | None,
102	        Argument(help="Task or dataset directories to publish."),
103	    ] = None,
104	    tag: Annotated[
105	        list[str] | None,
106	        Option(
107	            "--tag",
108	            "-t",
109	            help="Tag(s) to apply (repeatable). 'latest' is always added.",
110	        ),
111	    ] = None,
112	    concurrency: Annotated[
113	        int, Option("--concurrency", "-c", help="Max concurrent uploads.")
114	    ] = 50,
115	    no_tasks: Annotated[
116	        bool, Option("--no-tasks", help="Skip publishing tasks for datasets.")
117	    ] = False,
118	    public: Annotated[
119	        bool, Option("--public/--private", help="Set visibility (default: private).")
120	    ] = False,
121	    debug: Annotated[
122	        bool,
123	        Option(
124	            "--debug", help="Show extra timing details (e.g. RPC time).", hidden=True
125	        ),
126	    ] = False,
127	) -> None:
128	    """Publish tasks and datasets to the Harbor registry."""
129	    if paths is None:
130	        paths = [Path(".")]
131	
132	    from rich.console import Console
133	
134	    from harbor.cli.utils import run_async
135	    from harbor.publisher.publisher import Publisher
136	
137	    console = Console()
138	
139	    async def _publish() -> None:
140	        publisher = Publisher()
141	
142	        # Auth check
143	        try:
144	            await publisher.registry_db.get_user_id()
145	        except RuntimeError as exc:
146	            echo(str(exc))
147	            raise SystemExit(1)
148	
149	        task_dirs, dataset_dirs, explicit_task_dirs, explicit_dataset_dirs = (
150	            _resolve_paths(paths, no_tasks=no_tasks)
151	        )
152	        if not task_dirs and not dataset_dirs:
153	            echo("No tasks or datasets found.")
154	            raise SystemExit(1)
155	
156	        visibility = "public" if public else "private"
157	        tags = set(tag) if tag else None
158	        if task_dirs:
159	            await _publish_tasks(
160	                publisher,
161	                console,
162	                task_dirs,
163	                tags,
164	                concurrency,
165	                visibility,
166	                debug,
167	                explicit_task_dirs=explicit_task_dirs,
168	            )
169	        if dataset_dirs:
170	            await _publish_datasets(
171	                publisher,
172	                console,
173	                dataset_dirs,
174	                tags,
175	                visibility,
176	                explicit_dataset_dirs=explicit_dataset_dirs,
177	            )
178	
179	    try:
180	        run_async(_publish())
181	    except SystemExit:
182	        raise
183	    except ExceptionGroup as exc:
184	        echo(f"Error: {len(exc.exceptions)} sub-exception(s):")
185	        for index, sub_exc in enumerate(exc.exceptions, start=1):
186	            echo(f"  {index}. {_format_exception(sub_exc)}")
187	        if debug:
188	            raise
189	        raise SystemExit(1)
190	    except Exception as exc:
191	        echo(f"Error: {_format_exception(exc)}")
192	        if debug:
193	            raise
194	        raise SystemExit(1)
195	
196	
197	async def _publish_tasks(
198	    publisher: Publisher,
199	    console: Console,
200	    task_dirs: list[Path],
201	    tags: set[str] | None,
202	    concurrency: int,
203	    visibility: str,
204	    debug: bool = False,
205	    *,
206	    explicit_task_dirs: set[Path] | None = None,
207	) -> None:
208	    from rich.console import Group
209	    from rich.live import Live
210	    from rich.progress import (
211	        BarColumn,
212	        MofNCompleteColumn,
213	        Progress,
214	        SpinnerColumn,
215	        TextColumn,
216	        TimeElapsedColumn,
217	        TimeRemainingColumn,
218	    )
219	    from rich.progress import TaskID as ProgressTaskID
220	    from rich.table import Table
221	
222	    from harbor.publisher.publisher import PublishResult
223	
224	    overall_progress = Progress(
225	        SpinnerColumn(),
226	        MofNCompleteColumn(),
227	        TextColumn("[progress.description]{task.description}"),
228	        BarColumn(),
229	        TimeElapsedColumn(),
230	        TimeRemainingColumn(),
231	    )
232	    running_progress = Progress(
233	        SpinnerColumn(),
234	        TimeElapsedColumn(),
235	        TextColumn("[progress.description]{task.description}"),
236	    )
237	
238	    running_tasks: dict[str, ProgressTaskID] = {}
239	    explicit_result_names: list[str] = []
240	
241	    def on_start(task_dir: Path) -> None:
242	        running_tasks[str(task_dir)] = running_progress.add_task(
243	            f"{task_dir.name}: publishing...", total=None
244	        )
